detections.description: OpenAI domain-verification TXT (`openai-domain-verification=` prefix). Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a ChatGPT Team or Enterprise tenant, typically as the SSO and provisioning precondition. Does not prove active usage; the verification ships before users are licensed.

Anthropic domain-verification TXT for Claude for Work / Claude Enterprise tenant claim. Indicates an Anthropic workspace is bound to the domain. Does not by itself prove the workspace is in active use; verification is the SSO and provisioning hook.

Mistral AI enterprise domain-verification TXT (`mistral-domain-verification=` prefix). Indicates the domain is registered with a Mistral tenant for SAML SSO. Does not indicate which Mistral product line is in use; the verification is shared across the enterprise surface.

Perplexity Enterprise Pro domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is bound to a Perplexity enterprise workspace as the SSO-enablement step. Does not prove active seat usage; some orgs verify the domain during a trial without rolling out.

n8n workflow-automation domain-verification TXT for the n8n Cloud enterprise tier. Indicates an n8n Cloud workspace is bound to the domain. Does not distinguish self-hosted from managed n8n; this TXT is specific to the managed-cloud enterprise path.

Dify AI app-builder domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Dify workspace, typically for SSO or custom-domain hosted apps. Does not by itself indicate production deployment; many orgs verify during evaluation.

Microsoft AutoGen agentic-framework domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with AutoGen Studio or the AutoGen managed-service path. Does not prove the org's runtime agents reach external endpoints from this domain; the TXT is a tenant-claim signal, not a runtime signal.

CrewAI Agent Interface Discovery TXT at `_agent.<domain>`, advertising autonomous-agent MCP endpoints per the AID v1 proposal. Indicates the org is publishing discoverable agent surfaces. Does not prove the advertised endpoints are reachable or authenticated; AID is a discovery layer, not a contract.

CNAME delegation to LangSmith's managed-GCP infrastructure for AI observability and tracing. Indicates the domain routes LangSmith traffic through LangChain's hosted control plane. Does not prove which LangChain SDKs are emitting traces; the CNAME is the ingestion surface, not the application code.

Model Context Protocol DNS discovery record at `_mcp.<domain>`, advertising MCP server endpoints per the v1 discovery draft. Indicates the org is publishing MCP endpoints for agent discovery. Does not prove the advertised servers are reachable or implement the current MCP spec; treat as discovery metadata, not a contract.

Salesforce domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Salesforce org, typically for SSO federation or Experience Cloud custom-domain setup. Does not by itself identify which Salesforce product (Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Experience Cloud, Marketing Cloud) is in use.

Salesforce Organization ID (the `00D` prefix is the fixed identifier for Org records in Salesforce's object-prefix scheme). Indicates the domain has been verified to a specific Salesforce org. The OrgID itself is a unique tenant identifier; presence proves an administrative CRM relationship between the domain owner and a Salesforce org.

SPF include matching salesforce.com. Authorizes Salesforce to send mail on behalf of the domain, typically for Salesforce-initiated emails (case responses, Marketing Cloud sends through the platform, automated workflows).
Alternative Salesforce SPF include (`_spf.salesforce.com`). Same outbound-authorization semantics as the bare `salesforce.com` include; some tenant configurations use the underscored form.
CNAME pointing into sfmc-marketing.com, Salesforce Marketing Cloud's link-tracking and click-attribution endpoint. Indicates the domain has at least one tracked subdomain for SFMC sends.
CNAME pointing into sfmc-content.com, Salesforce Marketing Cloud's content and image hosting endpoint. Indicates the domain serves SFMC-hosted images and attachments through a branded subdomain.

CNAME pointing into exacttarget.com, the legacy domain for Salesforce Marketing Cloud (originally ExactTarget, acquired by Salesforce in 2013). Functionally equivalent to the `sfmc-*.com` patterns; some tenants still use the legacy CNAME.

HubSpot domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a HubSpot account, typically for hosting HubSpot-served landing pages, blog posts, or marketing sends under a branded subdomain. Does not identify which HubSpot Hubs (Marketing, Sales, Service, CMS) are licensed.

HubSpot developer-portal or sandbox-account domain verification TXT. Used for verifying domains against HubSpot's developer test accounts rather than production. Presence suggests a development team is using HubSpot tooling; less indicative of production HubSpot use.
SPF include matching hubspotemail.net. Authorizes HubSpot to send marketing and transactional emails on behalf of the domain. Typically present alongside the HubSpot domain-verification TXT.

CNAME pointing into hubspot.net, HubSpot's CDN and hosted-page endpoint. Indicates the domain serves at least one page (landing page, blog, or knowledge base) from HubSpot infrastructure under a branded subdomain.

SPF include matching mktomail.com, Marketo's outbound sending infrastructure (Marketo is now part of Adobe Experience Cloud after the 2018 acquisition). Authorizes Marketo to send marketing email on behalf of the domain.
CNAME pointing into mktosvc.com, Marketo's hosted service endpoint for tracking and link redirects. Indicates the domain uses Marketo for marketing campaigns.
CNAME pointing into mktoweb.com, Marketo's landing-page hosting endpoint. Indicates the domain hosts Marketo landing pages under a branded subdomain.

Klaviyo site-verification TXT (Klaviyo is an e-commerce- focused email and SMS marketing platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Klaviyo account, typically for branded sending domains and SMS short-link tracking. Common in Shopify and other e-commerce stacks.

CNAME pointing into kartra.com (Kartra is an all-in-one marketing platform offering landing pages, email, checkout, and membership sites). Indicates the domain hosts at least one Kartra-served page or funnel.

LiveRamp site-verification TXT (LiveRamp is a data connectivity and identity-resolution platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a LiveRamp account. Confidence is medium because the verification can persist independent of active data-onboarding use.
Segment site-verification TXT (Segment is a Twilio-owned customer-data platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Segment workspace.

CNAME pointing into segment.com. Typically a tracking subdomain delegated to Segment for client-side event collection through a branded endpoint. Does not prove Segment is the org's primary CDP; many tenants run Segment alongside an in-house analytics pipeline. The legacy `segment.io` chain is captured separately.
CNAME pointing into segment.io (the legacy Segment tracking domain pre-Twilio acquisition). Functionally equivalent to segment.com; some tenants still use the legacy CNAME.

Meta (Facebook) Business Suite domain-verification TXT (the `facebook-domain-verification=` prefix predates the Meta rebrand). Indicates the domain has been claimed by a Meta Business account, typically for ad-account ownership, Aggregated Event Measurement, or Conversions API setup. Does not prove active ad campaigns are running.

Yandex domain-verification TXT (Yandex is a Russian internet-services company offering search, ads, and cloud). Indicates the domain is verified to a Yandex account, typically for Yandex Webmaster, Yandex Direct ads, or Yandex Metrica analytics. Confidence is medium because the verification persists across the various Yandex products and does not identify which one is in use.

CNAME pointing into unbounce.com (Unbounce is a landing-page builder and conversion-optimization platform). Indicates the domain hosts at least one Unbounce-served landing page under a branded subdomain.

CNAME pointing into pardot.com (Pardot is Salesforce's B2B marketing-automation product, rebranded to Marketing Cloud Account Engagement in 2022 but the pardot.com hostname is still in service). Indicates the domain hosts Pardot-served landing pages or tracking endpoints.

CNAME pointing into drift.com (Drift is a conversational- marketing and chatbot platform, acquired by Salesloft in 2024). Indicates the domain hosts Drift's chat widget or related endpoints. Confidence is medium because the CNAME can persist after a team has moved off Drift.

CNAME pointing into braze.com (Braze is a customer- engagement platform offering email, push, in-app, and SMS messaging). Indicates the domain uses Braze for at least one channel, typically with a branded tracking subdomain.

CNAME pointing into iterable.com (Iterable is a cross-channel marketing-automation platform). Indicates the domain uses Iterable for marketing sends, typically with a branded tracking or link-rewrite subdomain.
CNAME pointing into customer.io (Customer.io is a behavioral-messaging platform for email, push, and SMS). Indicates the domain uses Customer.io for at least one messaging channel.
CNAME pointing into 6sense.com (6sense is an account- based marketing and B2B intent-data platform). Indicates the domain uses 6sense for tracking, typically through a branded tracking subdomain.
CNAME pointing into 6sense.io (alternative 6sense tracking domain). Same semantics as the .com form; some tenants use the .io variant.

Catch-all TXT pattern starting with "6sense". Lower- specificity fallback for 6sense verification records that do not follow a fixed prefix. Subject to false positives on TXT records mentioning 6sense in unrelated contexts.

CNAME pointing into demandbase.com (Demandbase is an account-based marketing platform that overlaps with 6sense in B2B intent data). Indicates the domain uses Demandbase, typically for site personalization or ABM campaigns.

CNAME pointing into eloqua.com (Eloqua is Oracle's marketing-automation platform, part of Oracle CX after the 2012 acquisition). Indicates the domain hosts Eloqua-served landing pages, forms, or tracking endpoints.

CNAME pointing into clearbit.com (Clearbit is a data-enrichment platform, acquired by HubSpot in 2023 and now branded as Breeze Intelligence). Indicates the domain uses Clearbit for visitor identification or enrichment, typically through a branded API subdomain.
Catch-all TXT pattern starting with "outreach" (Outreach is a sales-engagement platform). Lower-specificity fallback for Outreach verification records.
SPF include matching outreach.io. Authorizes Outreach to send sales engagement email on behalf of the domain.
Catch-all TXT pattern starting with "salesloft" (Salesloft is a sales-engagement platform, comparable to Outreach). Lower-specificity fallback for Salesloft verification records.
SPF include matching salesloft.com. Authorizes Salesloft to send sales engagement email on behalf of the domain.
Apollo.io domain-verification TXT (Apollo is a sales- intelligence and engagement platform offering contact data and outbound automation). Indicates the domain is registered with an Apollo account.
Gong domain-verification TXT (Gong is a revenue- intelligence platform that records and analyzes sales calls). Indicates the domain is registered with a Gong account.
CNAME pointing into intercom.io (Intercom is a customer- messaging and support-chat platform). Indicates the domain hosts Intercom's messenger widget or related endpoints under a branded subdomain.
Intercom domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with an Intercom workspace, typically for custom email domains used by Intercom-sent campaign or transactional messages.

Microsoft Dynamics 365 Marketing domain-verification TXT (the `d365mktkey=` prefix). Indicates the domain is registered with a Dynamics 365 Marketing tenant for use as a sending domain in marketing journeys. Distinct from base Dynamics 365 CRM verification.

Salesforce Marketing Cloud domain-verification TXT (the `SFMC-` prefix). Indicates the domain is verified to a Salesforce Marketing Cloud account for use as a sending domain. Distinct slug from the broader `salesforce-mc` CNAME-based detections; this is the explicit verification TXT.

Emma email marketing domain-verification TXT (the `e2ma-verification=` prefix; e2ma is Emma's legacy internal name). Emma is now part of Marigold, which consolidated Emma, Campaign Monitor, and other mid-market email vendors. Indicates the domain is registered with an Emma account.

iContact email marketing domain-verification TXT (iContact is a J2 Global / Ziff Davis property focused on small-business email marketing). Indicates the domain is registered with an iContact account.
MailerLite domain-verification TXT (MailerLite is a mid-market email marketing platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a MailerLite account, typically for use as a branded sending domain.

Databricks Lakehouse Platform domain-verification TXT token. Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Databricks workspace, typically for SSO, account claim, or enterprise identity setup. Does not prove active query workload. Many orgs verify the domain during initial setup; corroborate with observed user or data flows before reading this as "actively running on Databricks."

Snowflake data-warehouse domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain has been claimed for SSO or SCIM provisioning against a Snowflake account. Does not identify which Snowflake region or cloud (AWS, Azure, or GCP) the account lives in, and does not indicate the warehouse is actively queried. The token persists after the verification step.

MongoDB Atlas domain-verification TXT token. Indicates the domain has been registered with an Atlas organization, typically for SSO federation or custom-domain endpoints. Does not prove any specific database is hosted on Atlas. The verification can be set up before any cluster exists.

Dynatrace domain-verification TXT (Dynatrace is an observability and APM platform). Indicates the domain is claimed by a Dynatrace tenant. Does not prove the domain's services are actively instrumented. Agent rollout is independent of the domain verification.

Datadog domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Datadog organization, used for SSO identity-provider federation and custom RUM or synthetics endpoints. Does not identify which Datadog site (US1, US3, US5, EU, and so on) the org is on, and does not prove active monitoring of this specific domain's infrastructure.

New Relic domain-verification TXT (New Relic is an APM and observability platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a New Relic account, typically for SSO. Confidence is medium because the verification token can persist long after the account becomes inactive. Corroborate via APM agent traffic or insights URLs before reading this as "currently using New Relic."

CNAME pointing into walkme.com (WalkMe is a digital-adoption platform that hosts in-app guidance and onboarding overlays). Indicates the domain serves at least one page with WalkMe injected. Common false positive: a CNAME for a single legacy training subdomain does not mean WalkMe is in active production use across the apex.

CNAME pointing into optimizely.com (Optimizely is an experimentation and A-B-testing platform). Indicates at least one page or feature flag served via Optimizely's edge. Does not distinguish between Optimizely's Web Experimentation, Feature Experimentation, or CMP products on the same vendor; confidence is medium for that reason.

CNAME pointing into mixpanel.com (Mixpanel is a product- analytics vendor). Indicates the domain proxies analytics events through Mixpanel-hosted infrastructure, typically via a `mixpanel-proxy.example.com` subdomain. Does not prove client-side Mixpanel SDK presence on user-facing pages; that ships in JavaScript and is not observable via DNS.

Amplitude product-analytics domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with an Amplitude organization, typically for SSO federation or custom-domain ingestion. Does not prove active event ingestion. Verification is a one-time setup step.

CNAME pointing into heapanalytics.com (Heap is an auto-capture product-analytics vendor). Indicates the domain proxies events through Heap's infrastructure. Does not indicate which Heap edition (free, standard, or enterprise) or whether the SDK is currently loaded on user-facing pages.

CNAME pointing into pendo.io (Pendo offers in-app guides and product analytics). Indicates the domain serves at least one page with Pendo embedded. Common with SaaS apps using Pendo for user-onboarding overlays. Does not distinguish Pendo's Engage (in-app guides) from its analytics-only offering on the same vendor.

Hotjar heatmap and session-replay domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Hotjar account. Confidence is medium because the verification persists regardless of whether the tracking script is currently installed on user-facing pages. DNS cannot observe client-side script presence.

Oracle Email Delivery SPF include (`oracleemaildelivery.com`). Bulk-mail delivery service hosted on Oracle Cloud. Domains that include this in their SPF use Oracle for outbound transactional or marketing email.

PowerSPF (SPF flattening service) include (`powerspf.com`). A flattener consolidates many third-party SPF includes into a single dynamically-generated record. Indicates the org uses a managed SPF rather than a hand-maintained record.
Sailthru (Marigold) SPF include (`sailthru.com`). Sailthru is a CRM / email marketing platform; an SPF include here means the org sends marketing email through Sailthru.
Amadeus (travel technology) SPF include (`amadeus.com`). Common on airline, hotel, and travel agency apexes for booking and itinerary email.
Elastic Email SPF include (`elasticemail.com`). Bulk transactional and marketing email delivery service.
Sage Intacct (cloud accounting) SPF include (`intacct.com`). Email originating from this domain is dispatched by the Intacct tenant on the org's behalf.
Constant Contact SPF include (`constantcontact.com`). Email marketing platform; SPF include indicates outbound campaigns are dispatched through Constant Contact.
spf-report (SPF flattening / reporting service) include (`spf-report.com`). A managed SPF service. Indicates the org relies on a flattener rather than a hand-maintained record.
Everbridge (critical-event / mass notification) SPF include (`everbridge.net`). Indicates the org uses Everbridge for emergency or operational notifications.
Tipalti (accounts-payable automation) SPF include (`tipalti.com`). Tipalti dispatches supplier and payment-related email on the org's behalf.
MessageProvider (transactional email) SPF include (`messageprovider.com`). Indicates outbound email is dispatched through MessageProvider.
MailChannels SPF include (`mailchannels.net`). Outbound SMTP relay commonly used by hosting providers and white-labelled mail services.
Braintree (PayPal payment gateway) SPF include (`braintreegateway.com`). Indicates Braintree dispatches transaction email on the org's behalf.
Stibee (Korean email marketing) SPF include (`stibee.com`). Common on Korean-market apexes for newsletter delivery.
Ipreo (IHS Markit investor-relations communications) SPF include (`ipreomail.com`). Common on public-company apexes for IR shareholder email.
SMTP.com (transactional email service) SPF include (`smtp.com`). Indicates outbound mail is dispatched through SMTP.com's infrastructure.
Mxrecord email-delivery MX target (`mxrecord.mx`). Indicates the org routes inbound mail through Mxrecord.
Fastmail MX target (`messagingengine.com`). Indicates the org uses Fastmail as its inbound mail provider.
Hornetsecurity (email security gateway) MX target (`hornetsecurity.com`). Indicates inbound mail is filtered by Hornetsecurity.
AppRiver / Zix email-security gateway MX target (`arsmtp.com`). Indicates inbound mail is filtered by AppRiver before delivery.
TitanHQ (SpamTitan email security) MX target (`titanhq.com`). Indicates inbound mail is filtered by SpamTitan.
Apple iCloud Mail MX target (`icloud.com`). Indicates the org uses iCloud (or iCloud Custom Email Domain) for inbound mail.
Iberlayer Mailguard (Spanish email security) MX target (`iberlayer.com`). Common on Iberian-market apexes for inbound mail filtering.
Cloudflare Email Routing / DMARC aggregate-report destination (`cloudflare.net`). The org uses Cloudflare's DMARC analytics to collect aggregate reports.
U.S. CISA / DHS DMARC aggregate-report destination (`dhs.gov`). The org has bound its DMARC rua to the federal compliance pipeline (BOD 18-01 or a successor). Common on .gov and contractor apexes.
Skout / sdmarc DMARC aggregate-report destination (`sdmarc.net`). DMARC-as-a-service vendor; rua there indicates the org outsources DMARC analytics.
cp-dmarc (DMARC analytics) aggregate-report destination (`cp-dmarc.com`). Indicates the org pipes rua reports to cp-dmarc.
EmailAnalyst (DMARC analytics) aggregate-report destination (`emailanalyst.com`).
Red Sift OnDMARC aggregate-report destination (`redsift.cloud`). Red Sift offers managed DMARC monitoring and enforcement.
Report URI (security report aggregation) DMARC destination (`report-uri.com`). Report URI also handles CSP, HPKP, and NEL reports.
DMARC360 aggregate-report destination (`dmarc360.com`).
MailHardener (DMARC / SPF / DKIM monitoring) DMARC aggregate- report destination (`mailhardener.com`).
DMARC25 (Japanese DMARC analytics) aggregate-report destination (`dmarc25.jp`).
InboxMonster (deliverability monitoring) DMARC aggregate-report destination (`inboxmonster.com`).
DMARCInput (DMARC analytics) aggregate-report destination (`dmarcinput.com`).
GlockApps (deliverability testing) DMARC aggregate-report destination (`glockapps.com`).

Atlassian sending-domain TXT (`atlassian-sending-domain-verification=`). Confirms outbound Atlassian email (Jira / Confluence notifications) from this domain. Account-binding signal only.
Google Workspace recovery-binding TXT (`google-gws-recovery-domain-verification=`). Used during Workspace account-recovery flow binding. Account-binding signal only.
Google work-accounts domain-binding TXT (`work-accounts-domain-verification=`). Account-binding signal only.
Smartsheet Gov tenant verification TXT (`smartsheet-gov-site-validation=`). Indicates the org uses Smartsheet's FedRAMP-certified instance.
Pexip portal domain-verification TXT (`pexip-portal-domain-verification=`). Indicates the org uses Pexip's customer portal.
Aliyun (Alibaba Cloud) site-verification TXT (`aliyun-site-verification=`). Confirms the org has a domain bound to an Aliyun (China-region) account.
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) domain-verification TXT (`oci-domain-verification=`). Account-binding signal only.
Brevo (formerly Sendinblue) site-verification TXT (`sendinblue-site-verification=`). Account-binding signal only.
Cisco Secure Email domain-verification TXT (`cisco-site-verification=`). Account-binding signal only.
Intercom domain-validation TXT (`intercom-domain-validation=`). Account-binding signal only.
Heroku domain-verification TXT (`heroku-domain-verification=`). Indicates a custom domain bound to a Heroku app or team.
GitHub organization domain-verification TXT (`github-verification=`). Indicates the domain is verified for a GitHub organization.
Zendesk domain-verification TXT (`zendeskverification=`). Account-binding signal only.
Loom domain-verification TXT (`loom-verification=`). Account- binding signal only. (Variant of the `loom-site-verification=` prefix added in v1.9.23.)
Mailgun ownership / sending-domain verification TXT (`mgverify=`). Common on Mailgun-authenticated sending domains.

Oracle Cloud generic SPF include (`oraclecloud.com`). Email sent from OCI services (e.g. APEX, Notifications) authenticates via this include.

GoDaddy hosted-email SPF include (`secureserver.net`). Outbound mail dispatched through GoDaddy hosting.
Microsoft 365 GCC / GCC-High SPF include (`spf.protection.office365.us`). U.S. government tenants.
Google Workspace MX target (`smtp.google.com`). Variant of the legacy `aspmx.l.google.com` series; used by Workspace tenants with the modernized delivery edge.
AWS SES inbound MX target in `us-east-1`. Specific region hostname; non-us-east-1 tenants are captured by region- specific siblings below.
AWS SES inbound MX target in `us-west-2`.
AWS SES inbound MX target in `eu-west-1`.
AWS SES inbound MX target in `eu-central-1`.
AWS SES inbound MX target in `ap-southeast-1`.
AWS SES inbound MX target in `ap-northeast-1`.
Microsoft 365 modernized MX target (`*.mx.microsoft`).
Microsoft 365 null-MX placeholder (`msv1.invalid`). Tenants in mid-migration / disabled inbound state.
Microsoft 365 Exchange Online MX edge (`*.eo.outlook.com`).
Trellix / FireEye Email Security Gov MX target (`fireeyegov.com`).
Fortinet FortiMail Cloud MX target (`fortimailcloud.com`).
Trend Micro Email Security gateway MX target (`trendmicro.com`).
Alibaba Mail / DingTalk Mail MX target (`alibaba-inc.com`).
ProtonMail MX target (`protonmail.ch`). Variant of the `mail.protonmail.ch` series.
CSC Global brand-protection mail relay MX target (`cscdns.net`).
Cisco Secure Email Reporting / DMARC rua destination (`dmp.cisco.com`).
Barracuda Email Protection DMARC rua destination (`barracudanetworks.com`).
Brevo DMARC reporting destination (`dmarc.brevo.com`).
Trend Micro Email Security EU DMARC reporting destination (`trendmicro.eu`).
Mailgun DMARC aggregate-report destination (`mailgun.org`).
GoDaddy DMARC reporting destination (`onsecureserver.net`).
Alibaba Cloud DMARC reporting destination (`service.alibaba.com`).

Google Domains NS target (`googledomains.com`). Indicates authoritative DNS hosting on the legacy Google Domains namespace, now operated by Squarespace. It does not establish Google Workspace or a Google identity tenant.
Oracle Cloud authoritative DNS NS target (`oraclecloud.net`).
MarkMonitor authoritative DNS NS target (`markmonitor.zone`). Modern brand-protection DNS edge.
GoDaddy DefensiveDNS NS target (`gdnsdef.com`). Premium DDoS- protected DNS.
Netlify-hosted custom domain CNAME target (`netlify.com`).
Microsoft 365 hybrid / federated mail CNAME target (`outlook.com`). Common on autodiscover / pod hostnames.
Heroku custom-DNS CNAME target (`herokuspace.com`). Customer- configured app hostnames.
Alibaba Cloud authoritative DNS CNAME endpoint (`alibabadns.com`).
Alibaba Cloud generic CNAME target (`alibaba.com`).
AWS apps suite (WorkMail / SES web console) CNAME target (`awsapps.com`).
Zoho hosted-app CNAME target (`zohohost.com`).
Cloudflare China partner CNAME target (`pacloudflare.com`). Used by tenants on Cloudflare's China-network partnership.
Salesforce Desk (legacy support product) CNAME target (`.desk.com`). Leading dot scopes the match so it does not shadow `zendesk.com` / `mynewsdesk.com` via substring matching.
Cvent Lanyon / LWCal event microsite CNAME target (`lwcal.com`).
MuleSoft CloudHub iPaaS-hosted endpoint CNAME target (`cloudhub.io`).
StackPath / MaxCDN legacy CDN CNAME target (`netdna-cdn.com`).
Etouches / Stova event-registration CNAME target (`etouches.com`). Legacy Etouches brand, now Stova.
Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) Cloud Service / CQ-managed CNAME target (`adobecqms.net`).

Microsoft 365 domain-verification TXT (the `MS=ms########` form Microsoft issues during tenant domain claim). Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Microsoft 365 or Entra tenant. Does not prove the tenant is actively used for email. Some orgs verify the domain to claim the namespace then route mail elsewhere, so corroborate with MX evidence before reading this as "they run M365 email."

Alternative Microsoft 365 verification TXT format (the `ms-domain-verification=` prefix used for some Entra ID and Microsoft Defender services). Same administrative- binding semantics as `MS=ms########`. Does not by itself imply active email use.

MX record terminating at Microsoft 365's mail-protection cluster (`*.mail.protection.outlook.com`). Indicates active M365 email use: incoming SMTP for the domain routes through Exchange Online Protection. Note that an upstream gateway (Proofpoint, Mimecast, Barracuda) may sit in front of M365 even when this MX is published, so "M365 only" is not implied without checking for gateway MX evidence as well.

SPF `include:spf.protection.outlook.com` authorizes Microsoft 365 to send mail on behalf of the domain. Typically present alongside the M365 MX and consistent with M365 use. Does not prove M365 is the primary sender; many domains include M365 as one of several authorized senders.

MX terminating at Google Workspace's primary inbound mail cluster (`aspmx.l.google.com` and the `alt1` through `alt4` variants). Indicates active Google Workspace email use. As with M365, an upstream gateway may sit in front of Gmail even when this MX is published; check for gateway MX evidence before concluding "Gmail only."

SPF `include:_spf.google.com` authorizes Google to send mail on behalf of the domain. Usually present with the GWS MX and consistent with Google Workspace use. Does not distinguish Workspace from consumer Gmail forwarding setups on its own; combined with the MX, the read is GWS.

Zoho domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is claimed by a Zoho account (Zoho Mail, CRM, and other Zoho One services share the same verification mechanism). Does not distinguish Zoho Mail from other Zoho products on its own; corroborate with Zoho MX for the email claim specifically.
MX terminating at Zoho Mail (`mx*.zoho.com` and regional variants like `mx*.zohomail.eu`). Indicates Zoho Mail is the active mail provider for the domain.

Proton Mail custom-domain verification TXT. Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Proton account for use as a Proton Mail custom-domain alias. Does not prove Proton Mail is the exclusive mail provider for the domain; corroborate with Proton MX records (`*.protonmail.ch`).

Amazon SES domain-verification TXT (the `amazonses:` prefix). Indicates the domain is verified to send mail through Amazon SES, typically for transactional or application-generated email. SES is rarely the inbound mail provider, so combined with a non-SES MX the more accurate read is "uses SES for outbound application mail."

SPF `include:amazonses.com` authorizes Amazon SES to send mail on behalf of the domain. Typically a sender-only authorization for transactional mail; not by itself evidence of inbound mail handling on SES.

SPF `include:sendgrid.net` authorizes SendGrid (a Twilio property) to send mail on behalf of the domain. SendGrid is a transactional and bulk-mail sender; this is an outbound-authorization signal, not an indication that SendGrid receives mail for the domain.
SPF `include:mailgun.org` authorizes Mailgun (a Sinch property) to send mail on behalf of the domain. Transactional and API-driven outbound sender; the signal is outbound only.

SPF `include:spf1.sendcloud.org` authorizes SendCloud (a transactional and marketing-mail sender common in EU and Asia-Pacific markets) to send on behalf of the domain. Note that the name collides with multiple unrelated "SendCloud" companies; this fingerprint covers the transactional-mail vendor at sendcloud.net, not the EU shipping platform of the same name.
Alternative SendCloud SPF include (`spf2`), used alongside `spf1` for SPF lookup-count optimization. Same outbound- authorization semantics.
SPF include matching against Postmark (an ActiveCampaign property, formerly Wildbit). Authorizes Postmark to send transactional mail on behalf of the domain. Outbound-only signal.

SPF include matching against SparkPost's `sparkpostmail.com` (a MessageBird and Bird property). Authorizes SparkPost to send on behalf of the domain. Outbound transactional and marketing mail; not inbound mail handling.

Legacy Sendinblue domain-verification TXT (the brand rebranded to Brevo, but pre-rebrand verifications still use the `Sendinblue-code` prefix). Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Brevo account for marketing-mail sending. Does not prove active sending volume; the verification persists post-rebrand.
Post-rebrand Brevo (formerly Sendinblue) domain- verification TXT. Same administrative-binding semantics as the legacy `Sendinblue-code` form. New verifications use this prefix.

SPF `include:servers.mcsv.net` authorizes Mailchimp (an Intuit property) to send marketing mail on behalf of the domain. The `mcsv` stands for "Mailchimp Servers"; the include predates the Intuit acquisition.

SPF `include:spf.mandrillapp.com` authorizes Mandrill (Mailchimp's transactional-mail product) to send on behalf of the domain. Distinct product from Mailchimp's marketing mail; presence indicates transactional or API- driven mail use, not necessarily marketing campaigns.

SMTP TLS Reporting (RFC 8460) policy TXT, found at `_smtp._tls.<domain>`. Indicates the domain has opted in to receive aggregated TLS failure reports for inbound SMTP connections. Implies a baseline of email-security tooling investment; does not by itself prove DMARC enforcement.

SPF include matching AutoSPF, a flattening and lookup-count management service. Indicates the domain uses an external SPF management product, common in enterprise mail setups with many authorized senders.
SPF include matching OnDMARC (Red Sift's email- authentication-management product). Indicates the domain uses OnDMARC for SPF or DMARC management.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at OnDMARC (Red Sift). Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to OnDMARC for analysis.
SPF include matching dmarcian, a DMARC-management and SPF-optimization vendor. Indicates the domain uses dmarcian's tooling.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at dmarcian. Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to dmarcian for analysis.
SPF include matching EasyDMARC, an email-authentication- management vendor. Indicates the domain uses EasyDMARC's tooling.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at EasyDMARC. Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to EasyDMARC for analysis.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at EasyDMARC's US reporting endpoint (`*.easydmarc.us`). Same vendor as the .com pattern.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at EasyDMARC's EU reporting endpoint (`*.easydmarc.eu`). Same vendor as the .com pattern.
SPF include matching Valimail, an email-authentication- automation and SPF-management vendor. Indicates the domain uses Valimail's tooling.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at Valimail. Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to Valimail for analysis.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at Valimail's shortened reporting domain (`*.vali.email`). Same vendor as the valimail.com pattern.
Valimail Gov DMARC rua destination (`valigov.email`). Government tenants. Same vendor as the vali.email pattern.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at Agari (a Fortra property after the Helpsystems / Fortra acquisitions). Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to Agari for analysis.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at Proofpoint Email Fraud Defense. Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to Proofpoint EFD, typically alongside Proofpoint gateway use.

DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at URIports. Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to URIports for analysis. URIports also handles MTA-STS reporting and CSP violation reports for other security headers; the slug is DMARC-specific.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at DMARC Advisor. Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to DMARC Advisor for analysis.
DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at PowerDMARC. Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports to PowerDMARC for analysis.

DMARC `rua=` aggregate-report target at Mimecast DMARC Analyzer (formerly the standalone DMARC Analyzer vendor acquired by Mimecast). Indicates the domain sends DMARC aggregate reports for analysis; commonly seen alongside Mimecast gateway use.

GitHub domain-verification TXT (GitHub is a Microsoft property since the 2018 acquisition). Indicates the domain is verified to a GitHub organization, typically a precondition for using verified-domain features like domain-restricted SAML SSO or Verified Domain badges on org profiles. Compare with `_github-pages-challenge` for the Pages-specific verification.

GitHub Pages domain-challenge TXT (found at `_github-pages-challenge-<org>.<domain>`). Indicates the domain has been verified for use as a custom Pages site. Distinct from org-level domain verification; Pages verification is per-site.

GitLab Pages domain-verification TXT at zone apex. Indicates the domain is verified for use as a custom GitLab Pages site. Common on GitLab SaaS (gitlab.com); self-managed GitLab installations can also use this verification.

GitLab Pages subdomain-verification TXT (found at a designated `_gitlab-pages-verification-code.<subdomain>` record). Same purpose as the apex form; used when the Pages site lives at a subdomain rather than the apex.

Postman domain-verification TXT (Postman is an API platform with collections, collaboration, and an API gateway). Indicates the domain is registered with a Postman team for SSO federation or custom-domain API documentation.

Linear domain-verification TXT (Linear is an issue- tracking and project-management platform popular in engineering organizations). Indicates the domain is registered with a Linear workspace, typically for SSO federation.

Autodesk domain-verification TXT (Autodesk publishes AutoCAD, Revit, Fusion 360, and the broader AEC and manufacturing design suite). Indicates the domain is registered with an Autodesk enterprise account.

Grammarly domain-verification TXT (Grammarly Business is the enterprise writing-assistance offering). Indicates the domain is registered with a Grammarly Business account, typically for SSO federation.

Vercel domain-verification TXT (Vercel is a frontend cloud platform best known as the Next.js maintainer). Indicates the domain is registered with a Vercel team for deploying applications under a custom domain.

CNAME pointing into vercel-dns.com. Indicates the domain uses Vercel's hosted DNS, typically when the domain was registered through Vercel or delegated to Vercel's nameservers for custom-domain management.

Webflow domain-verification TXT (Webflow is a no-code website-builder and CMS platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Webflow account, typically for hosting a Webflow site under the custom domain.
Netlify domain-verification TXT (the bare `netlify=` prefix). Indicates the domain is registered with a Netlify team for hosting one or more sites under the custom domain.
Netlify site-ownership verification TXT (`verified-for-netlify=` prefix). Used for per-site verification rather than team-level claim. Same administrative-binding semantics, finer-grained scope.
CNAME pointing into netlify.app. Indicates the domain hosts a Netlify-deployed site under a custom subdomain that proxies to Netlify's edge.

Twilio domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Twilio account, typically for SSO federation or for verifying a sending domain for Twilio SendGrid or Twilio Conversations. Does not identify which Twilio product is in use.

Citrix domain-verification TXT (Citrix is now part of Cloud Software Group after the 2022 merger with TIBCO). Indicates the domain is registered with a Citrix Cloud tenant, typically for SSO federation across Citrix DaaS, Citrix Workspace, or NetScaler.
Dell Technologies domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Dell Technologies enterprise account, typically for Dell premier or support-portal SSO federation.

CloudHealth domain-verification TXT (CloudHealth is a cloud cost-management platform, originally a VMware acquisition, now part of Broadcom after the 2023 VMware acquisition). Indicates the domain is registered with a CloudHealth tenant.

Unity SSO domain-verification TXT (Unity Technologies publishes the Unity game engine and developer tools). Indicates the domain is registered with a Unity organization for SSO federation, common at game studios and digital-twin teams.
PagerDuty domain-verification TXT (PagerDuty is an incident-management and on-call platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a PagerDuty account, typically for SSO federation.

CNAME pointing into onrender.com (Render is a unified cloud-application platform comparable to Heroku and Fly.io). Indicates the domain hosts at least one service deployed on Render under a custom domain.
Render domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Render account, a precondition for using a custom domain on Render-deployed services.

Cloudflare domain-verification TXT (`cloudflare-verify=` prefix). Indicates the domain is verified to a Cloudflare account, used for some Cloudflare products (Cloudflare for Teams, Cloudflare Pages custom domains, and so on).

NS records terminate at Cloudflare nameservers. The domain uses Cloudflare as authoritative DNS, a common precondition for using Cloudflare's CDN, WAF, or DNS products on the apex. Does not by itself prove the apex sits behind Cloudflare's CDN; those are separate products on the same vendor.
CNAME containing the string `cloudflare`. Lower- specificity fallback for Cloudflare-product CNAMEs that do not match the more-specific patterns elsewhere in the catalog.

NS records contain `awsdns` (AWS Route 53 nameserver hostnames follow the `ns-*.awsdns-*.{com,net,org,co.uk}` pattern). Indicates the domain uses Route 53 as authoritative DNS. Does not by itself indicate the domain's workloads run on AWS; many domains use Route 53 for DNS while hosting elsewhere.

NS records contain `azure-dns` (Azure DNS nameserver hostnames follow the `ns*.azure-dns.{com,net,org,info}` pattern). Indicates the domain uses Azure DNS as authoritative DNS. Does not by itself indicate Azure hosts the domain's workloads.

NS records terminating at Google Cloud DNS nameservers (`ns-cloud-{a,b,c,d}*.googledomains.com` and `*.google.com` patterns). Indicates the domain uses Google Cloud DNS as authoritative DNS. Note that a Google-NS match does not by itself distinguish Cloud DNS from legacy Google Domains DNS or from Workspace-managed DNS; the underlying service is GCP Cloud DNS in the modern configuration.

NS records contain `ultradns` (UltraDNS is a managed- DNS service originally part of Neustar, now part of Vercara after the 2024 Neustar Security Services acquisition). Indicates the domain uses UltraDNS as authoritative DNS, common in enterprise and financial-services configurations.
NS records terminating at Dyn's managed DNS (`*.dynect.net`). Dyn is now part of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure after the 2016 acquisition. Indicates the domain uses Dyn as authoritative DNS.
NS records terminating in akamai.net. Indicates the domain uses Akamai as authoritative DNS, often paired with Akamai's CDN edge or DNS-based traffic management products.
NS records terminating in akam.net (alternative Akamai authoritative-DNS domain). Same authoritative-DNS semantics as the akamai.net form.
CNAME terminating in akamaiedge.net. Indicates the domain or subdomain proxies through Akamai's edge, typically for CDN or WAF services.
CNAME terminating in edgekey.net (Akamai's edge-key domain used for some product configurations). Same edge-proxying semantics as the akamaiedge.net form.
CNAME terminating in edgesuite.net (Akamai's EdgeSuite CDN and WAF product domain). Same edge-proxying semantics as the other Akamai edge-CNAME patterns.

NS records terminating in nsone.net (NS1 is a managed- DNS provider acquired by IBM in 2024). Indicates the domain uses NS1 as authoritative DNS, often selected for advanced traffic-steering and observability features.

NS records terminating in domaincontrol.com (GoDaddy's authoritative-DNS hostname). Indicates the domain is registered through GoDaddy and uses GoDaddy's default DNS hosting, which is common but not informative about where the domain's workloads actually run.

CNAME terminating in cloudfront.net (AWS CloudFront CDN distribution domain). Indicates the domain has at least one subdomain delegating to a CloudFront distribution. CloudFront is commonly used in front of S3, API Gateway, ELB, or custom origins.

CNAME terminating in azureedge.net (Azure CDN edge domain, covering both Azure CDN Standard from Microsoft and the deprecating Akamai and Verizon partner tiers). Indicates the domain delegates traffic to an Azure CDN profile.

CNAME terminating in azurewebsites.net (Azure App Service / Web Apps default hostname domain). Indicates the domain has at least one subdomain delegating to an App Service, which can host web apps, APIs, or Azure Functions in the App Service plan.

CNAME terminating in trafficmanager.net (Azure Traffic Manager DNS-based traffic routing). Indicates the domain uses Traffic Manager for DNS-level failover or geographic routing, typically in front of multi-region Azure deployments.
CNAME terminating in fastly.net (Fastly CDN edge domain). Indicates the domain delegates traffic to Fastly's edge, used for CDN, image optimization, and Compute@Edge workloads.

CNAME terminating in fastly.net or fastlylb.net (the latter is Fastly's load-balancer endpoint domain). Indicates the domain uses Fastly for CDN or edge compute. The combined-regex form covers configurations that use either domain.
CNAME terminating in myshopify.com (Shopify's storefront hostname domain). Indicates the domain points to a Shopify store, typically via a `shop.example.com` or apex CNAME flattening configuration.
CNAME terminating in wixdns.net (Wix's DNS hosting domain). Indicates the domain is hosted on Wix, the no-code website-builder platform. Common with small business and creator websites.
CNAME pointing into squarespace.com (Squarespace is a website-builder and e-commerce platform). Indicates the domain hosts at least one site or page on Squarespace.
CNAME terminating in herokuapp.com (Heroku is a Salesforce-owned application platform). Indicates the domain has at least one subdomain delegating to a Heroku dyno or formation.
CNAME pointing into wpengine.com (WP Engine is a managed-WordPress hosting platform). Indicates the domain hosts a WordPress site on WP Engine infrastructure.
WP Engine domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is verified for use on a WP Engine installation, typically a precondition for SSL provisioning on the managed-WordPress site.
CNAME pointing into pantheon.io (Pantheon is a managed-WordPress and managed-Drupal hosting platform). Indicates the domain hosts a Pantheon-managed site.

CNAME terminating in elb.amazonaws.com (AWS Application Load Balancer or Network Load Balancer classic endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates traffic to an AWS load balancer, typically fronting EC2, ECS, or EKS workloads.

CNAME containing `.elb.` (regional AWS ELB hostname patterns like `*.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com`). Catch-all for ALB / NLB / CLB endpoints across regions. Same workload-fronting semantics as the bare `elb.amazonaws.com` pattern.

CNAME terminating in s3.amazonaws.com (AWS S3 REST API endpoint). Indicates the domain proxies to an S3 bucket, typically for object storage or static-asset serving. Note that this does not by itself indicate static-website-hosting mode is enabled; for that, the `s3-website-*` patterns apply.

CNAME containing `.s3-website-` (AWS S3 static-website hosting endpoint, regional form like `s3-website-us-east-1.amazonaws.com`). Indicates the bucket is configured for static-website hosting and the domain serves through that endpoint.
CNAME containing `.s3-website.` (newer-style S3 static-website endpoint, like `s3-website.us-east-2.amazonaws.com`). Same static- website-hosting semantics as the dash-form pattern.
CNAME terminating in elasticbeanstalk.com (AWS Elastic Beanstalk PaaS endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates to a Beanstalk environment, typically a multi-tier application managed by Beanstalk.
CNAME terminating in appspot.com (Google App Engine's default hostname domain). Indicates the domain delegates to an App Engine service.

CNAME terminating in run.app (Google Cloud Run's default hostname domain). Indicates the domain delegates to a Cloud Run container service. Cloud Run is GCP's serverless container platform; presence of this CNAME suggests modern GCP usage patterns rather than legacy App Engine.

CNAME terminating in azurefd.net (Azure Front Door classic and Standard / Premium endpoint domain). Indicates the domain proxies through Azure Front Door, Microsoft's global load balancer and edge acceleration product.
CNAME terminating in afd.ms (Azure Front Door Standard and Premium short-form endpoint domain). Same Front Door semantics as the azurefd.net form; the .ms domain is used by some newer SKUs.

CNAME pointing into contentful.com (Contentful is a headless CMS). Indicates the domain proxies content through Contentful, typically via a `cdn.example.com` or `images.example.com` branded subdomain.
CNAME pointing into sanity.io (Sanity is a headless CMS with a real-time backend). Indicates the domain uses Sanity for content management, typically through a branded API or asset subdomain.
CNAME pointing into prismic.io (Prismic is a headless CMS). Indicates the domain uses Prismic for content management.

CNAME pointing into launchdarkly.com (LaunchDarkly is a feature-management and experimentation platform). Indicates the domain proxies feature-flag traffic through LaunchDarkly, often via a branded relay-proxy subdomain.

CNAME pointing into cloudinary.com (Cloudinary is an image and video management and delivery platform). Indicates the domain proxies media assets through Cloudinary, typically via a branded `images.example.com` subdomain.
CNAME pointing into imgix.net (imgix is a real-time image processing and delivery CDN). Indicates the domain proxies image assets through imgix.
CNAME pointing into keycdn.com (KeyCDN is a content delivery network). Indicates the domain proxies through KeyCDN's edge.
CNAME pointing into kxcdn.com (KeyCDN's edge-zone domain). Same CDN semantics as the keycdn.com form; kxcdn.com is the zone-hostname pattern.

CNAME terminating in stackpathdns.com (StackPath was a content-delivery and edge-compute platform that absorbed MaxCDN; StackPath shut down its CDN business in 2024 and many domains have migrated off). Indicates the domain delegated to StackPath's edge; the CNAME may persist on configurations not yet migrated.
CNAME pointing into ghost.io (Ghost is an open-source blogging and newsletter platform; ghost.io is the managed-hosting offering). Indicates the domain hosts a Ghost-managed publication.

CAA record authorizing Let's Encrypt to issue certificates for the domain. Indicates the domain owner has explicitly allow-listed Let's Encrypt as a certificate-issuing CA. Common with cost-conscious or automation-friendly TLS setups.

CAA record authorizing DigiCert to issue certificates for the domain. Indicates the domain owner has allow-listed DigiCert, an enterprise certificate authority used for OV and EV certificates and regulated industries.
CAA record authorizing Sectigo to issue certificates for the domain. Sectigo is a CA spun out from the Comodo CA business in 2018. Common in enterprise and mid-market TLS setups.

CAA record authorizing the Comodo CA (now Sectigo under the post-2018 rebrand). The comodoca.com authorization is functionally equivalent to sectigo.com; some CAA records still use the legacy identifier.

CAA record authorizing Amazon (typically AWS Certificate Manager) to issue certificates for the domain. Indicates the domain owner uses AWS-managed TLS, common with CloudFront, ELB, and API Gateway certificates.

CAA record authorizing Amazon Trust Services (Amazon's CA brand for the publicly-trusted certificates ACM issues). Functionally equivalent to the amazon.com authorization; some CAA records use the more-specific amazontrust.com identifier.

CAA record authorizing Google Trust Services to issue certificates for the domain. Indicates the domain owner uses Google-managed TLS, common with Google Cloud Load Balancing and managed-certificate setups on GCP.

CNAME terminating in fly.dev or edgeapp.net (Fly.io is a globally-distributed application platform). The fly.dev domain is the default-app hostname; edgeapp.net is the legacy form. Indicates the domain hosts at least one Fly.io-deployed service.
CNAME terminating in railway.app (Railway is a developer-focused deployment platform comparable to Render and Fly.io). Indicates the domain hosts at least one Railway-deployed service.

VMware Cloud domain-verification TXT (the `vmware-cloud-verification-` prefix). Indicates the domain is registered with a VMware Cloud account. VMware is now part of Broadcom after the 2023 acquisition; the VMware Cloud brand has been restructured but the verification prefix persists.

Sentry domain-verification TXT (Sentry is an error-tracking and APM platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Sentry organization, typically for SSO federation or for proxying SDK events through a branded subdomain.

Grafana Cloud site-verification TXT (Grafana Cloud is Grafana Labs' managed observability platform offering metrics, logs, traces, and profiles). Indicates the domain is registered with a Grafana Cloud organization.

Sumo Logic site-verification TXT (Sumo Logic is a cloud-native log analytics and observability platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Sumo Logic organization, typically for SSO federation.
Honeycomb.io site-verification TXT (Honeycomb is an observability platform specializing in high-cardinality tracing). Indicates the domain is registered with a Honeycomb account.
Statsig domain-verification TXT (Statsig is a feature- flag and experimentation platform comparable to LaunchDarkly and Split.io). Indicates the domain is registered with a Statsig project.
Split.io domain-verification TXT (Split is a feature- flag and experimentation platform, acquired by Harness in 2024). Indicates the domain is registered with a Split workspace.

Splunk domain-verification TXT. Used for Splunk Observability Cloud (formerly SignalFx) and Splunk Cloud Platform tenant claim. Indicates the domain is registered with a Splunk organization, typically for SSO federation.

CNAME terminating in signalfx.com (SignalFx was the observability product Splunk acquired in 2019, now sold as Splunk Observability Cloud; the signalfx.com domain persists for legacy ingestion endpoints). Indicates the domain uses Splunk Observability.

Atlassian Cloud organization domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is administratively bound to an Atlassian Cloud org (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, Atlassian Guard, and so on; products share the org-level verification). Common in setups with SSO federation and Atlassian Guard (formerly Atlassian Access).
Slack workspace domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is claimed by a Slack workspace, typically as part of Enterprise Grid setup. Slack is a Salesforce product after the 2021 acquisition.

Slack Enterprise Grid subdomain-challenge TXT found at `_slack-challenge.<domain>`. Indicates the domain is verified to a Slack Enterprise Grid org (Slack's top tier), not a Pro or Business+ workspace. Useful for distinguishing Enterprise customers from standard tenants.
Notion domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Notion workspace, typically for SSO federation or hosting Notion-published sites under a branded subdomain.
Alternative Notion verification TXT format (`notion-verification-code` prefix). Same administrative- binding semantics; Notion has used both forms over time.

Miro domain-verification TXT (Miro is a collaborative whiteboarding platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Miro team account, typically for SSO federation. Does not indicate active board volume.

Asana domain-verification TXT (Asana is a work-management platform for project and task tracking). Indicates the domain is registered with an Asana organization, typically for SSO federation or domain-restricted sharing.
Monday.com domain-verification TXT (monday.com is a work-management platform with workflow automation). Indicates the domain is registered with a monday.com account, typically for SSO federation.
Alternative monday.com verification TXT format. Same administrative-binding semantics as the prefixed `monday-domain-verification=` form.
Wrike domain-verification TXT (Wrike is a project- management platform acquired by Citrix and now part of Cloud Software Group). Indicates the domain is registered with a Wrike account.

Smartsheet site-validation TXT (Smartsheet is a spreadsheet-based work-management platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Smartsheet account, typically for SSO federation or branded sharing domains.
Airtable domain-verification TXT (Airtable is a hybrid database / spreadsheet collaboration platform). Indicates the domain is registered with an Airtable workspace, typically for SSO federation.
Alternative Airtable verification TXT format. Same administrative-binding semantics as the prefixed `airtable-domain-verification=` form.
ClickUp domain-verification TXT (ClickUp is a work- management platform combining project tracking, documents, and time tracking). Indicates the domain is registered with a ClickUp workspace.

Loom site-verification TXT (Loom is an asynchronous- video messaging platform, acquired by Atlassian in 2023). Indicates the domain is registered with a Loom account, typically for SSO federation in the Loom Business and Enterprise plans.
Canva site-verification TXT (Canva is a graphic-design platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Canva for Teams or Canva for Enterprise account, typically for SSO federation.

Beautiful.ai site-verification TXT (Beautiful.ai is a presentation-design platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Beautiful.ai account, typically for SSO federation in the team and enterprise plans.

CNAME pointing into disciplemedia.com (Disciple Media is a branded community platform for membership sites and private communities). Indicates the domain hosts a Disciple-served community under a branded subdomain.

Zoom domain-verification TXT (the `ZOOM_verify_` prefix). Indicates the domain is registered with a Zoom account, typically for Zoom SSO federation, Zoom Phone custom domains, or managed-domain claim. Does not indicate whether Zoom Meetings, Phone, Webinars, or Workplace is in use; the verification covers the broader Zoom account.

Dropbox domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Dropbox Business or Education team, typically for SSO federation and the team-managed-user claim. Does not by itself prove active file-storage use.

Workplace by Meta (formerly Workplace by Facebook) domain-verification TXT. Note that Meta is sunsetting Workplace by 2026 with full shutdown in 2026, so domains carrying this verification may be in active migration to Slack, Microsoft Teams, or other alternatives.

LucidLink domain-verification TXT (LucidLink is a cloud-native distributed file-system platform popular in media and entertainment production workflows). Indicates the domain is registered with a LucidLink organization.

Adobe Identity domain-verification TXT (the `adobe-idp` prefix corresponds to Adobe's Identity service used across Creative Cloud, Document Cloud, and Experience Cloud SSO). Indicates the domain is registered with an Adobe Admin Console organization for Federated ID or Enterprise ID setup.
Adobe Sign domain-verification TXT (Adobe Sign is the e-signature product within Adobe Document Cloud, formerly EchoSign). Indicates the domain is registered with an Adobe Sign tenant.

Apple Business / Apple Developer domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is claimed against an Apple enterprise account, typically for Apple Business Manager, Apple School Manager, or Sign in with Apple custom- domain setups. Does not distinguish which Apple service the verification is for.
Box domain-verification TXT (Box is an enterprise file storage and content collaboration platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Box enterprise account, typically for SSO federation.

Egnyte domain-verification TXT (Egnyte is an enterprise file-collaboration and content-governance platform that positions against Box, Dropbox Business, and SharePoint). Indicates the domain is registered with an Egnyte tenant.

Glean domain-verification TXT (Glean is an enterprise AI search and knowledge-management platform that indexes across SaaS applications). Indicates the domain is registered with a Glean tenant. Common in mid-to-large enterprises with sprawling SaaS stacks.
Zendesk domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Zendesk account, typically for branded help-center subdomains and SSO federation.
SPF include matching zendesk.com. Authorizes Zendesk to send customer-support emails (ticket notifications, help-center signups) on behalf of the domain.
Alternative Zendesk mail-sending SPF include (`mail.zendesk.com`). Some Zendesk tenants use this form. Same outbound-authorization semantics.
CNAME pointing into zendesk.com. Typically a branded help-center subdomain (`help.example.com` or `support.example.com`) hosted by Zendesk. Indicates active Zendesk use for customer support.
SPF include matching freshdesk.com (Freshdesk is a Freshworks helpdesk product, comparable to Zendesk). Authorizes Freshdesk to send support emails on behalf of the domain.

CNAME pointing into freshdesk.com. Typically a branded help-center subdomain hosted by Freshdesk. Does not by itself indicate ticket volume or which Freshworks products are bundled in the tenant; the chain is the hosting signal, not a usage signal.

SPF include matching service-now.com (ServiceNow is an ITSM, ITOM, and workflow platform). Authorizes ServiceNow to send platform-generated emails (incident notifications, approval requests) on behalf of the domain. Often appears in larger enterprise mail configurations.
Atlassian Statuspage domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Statuspage account for hosting a public status page under a branded subdomain.
CNAME pointing into statuspage.io. Typically a `status.example.com` subdomain delegated to Atlassian Statuspage for incident communication. Indicates the org operates a public-facing status page.

Workday domain-verification TXT (Workday is an enterprise HCM and finance platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Workday tenant. Common among large enterprises with full-suite HRIS deployments.

SAP domain-verification TXT (SAP is an enterprise software suite spanning ERP, HCM via SuccessFactors, CRM, and analytics). Indicates the domain is registered with an SAP cloud tenant. Does not identify which SAP product the verification is for.

SPF include matching `_spf.ultipro.com` (UKG, formerly Ultimate Software / UltiPro, merged with Kronos in 2020). Authorizes UKG to send HRIS-generated emails (paystub notifications, benefits enrollment) on behalf of the domain.

SPF include matching `_spf.qualtrics.com` (Qualtrics is an experience-management platform offering employee, customer, and product surveys; originally an SAP acquisition, spun out in 2023). Authorizes Qualtrics to send survey invitations on behalf of the domain.
Rippling domain-verification TXT (Rippling is an employee-management platform combining HR, IT provisioning, and finance). Indicates the domain is registered with a Rippling account.

Deel domain-verification TXT (Deel is a global-payroll and contractor-management platform). Indicates the domain is registered with a Deel account, typically for SSO federation in larger Deel deployments.

Figma domain-verification TXT (Figma is a collaborative design platform, currently independent after the blocked Adobe acquisition). Indicates the domain is registered with a Figma team or organization, typically for SSO federation.
Alternative Figma verification TXT format. Same administrative-binding semantics as the prefixed `figma-domain-verification=` form.

CrowdStrike Falcon domain-verification TXT (CrowdStrike is a cloud-delivered endpoint-protection vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a Falcon tenant. Does not prove sensor coverage on endpoints; sensor rollout is independent of the domain verification.

Catch-all TXT pattern containing the literal string "crowdstrike". This is corroborating evidence for the higher-specificity Falcon verification prefix, not a standalone detection path. Requiring both patterns suppresses unrelated TXT records that merely mention CrowdStrike in procurement notes, asset tags, or similar contexts.

KnowBe4 domain-verification TXT (KnowBe4 is a security- awareness and phishing-simulation training vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a KnowBe4 account, typically so that simulated-phishing campaigns can send from a tenant-owned domain. Does not by itself prove active training rollout.

SPF `include:_spf.psm.knowbe4.com` authorizes KnowBe4's PhishER / PhishER Plus mail-handling infrastructure to send mail on behalf of the domain. Typically present alongside the KnowBe4 verification TXT; useful as a second corroborating signal.

Proofpoint domain-verification TXT (Proofpoint is an email security gateway and CASB vendor). Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Proofpoint tenant. Does not prove the gateway sits in front of inbound mail; check MX evidence for that.

Less-specific Proofpoint TXT prefix, used by some Proofpoint product lines (Email Fraud Defense, Digital Risk Protection) for domain claim. Lower specificity than the `proofpoint-domain-verification=` form; the higher-specificity pattern is preferred when both fire.

MX terminating at Proofpoint's mail-protection cluster (`*.pphosted.com`, "PP hosted"). Indicates Proofpoint sits in front of inbound mail for the domain. Often observed in front of Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace tenants; the downstream provider is not deducible from this MX alone.
SPF `include:spf.pphosted.com` authorizes Proofpoint to send mail on behalf of the domain. Typically paired with the Proofpoint MX.
Alternative Proofpoint SPF include (`_spf.proofpoint.com`), used by some tenant configurations. Same authorization semantics as `spf.pphosted.com`.

Mimecast domain-verification TXT (Mimecast is an email security gateway vendor). Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a Mimecast tenant. Does not prove the gateway is currently in the inbound mail flow; check MX evidence for that.

MX terminating at Mimecast (`*.mimecast.com` regional variants like `eu-smtp-inbound.mimecast.com`). Indicates Mimecast sits in front of inbound mail. Like Proofpoint, often deployed in front of M365 or Google Workspace; the downstream provider is not deducible from this MX alone.
SPF `include:_netblocks.mimecast.com` authorizes Mimecast to send mail on behalf of the domain. Typically paired with the Mimecast MX.

MX terminating at Barracuda Networks' email-protection cloud (`*.barracudanetworks.com`). Indicates Barracuda Email Protection sits in front of inbound mail. The downstream mail provider is not deducible from this MX alone.
MX terminating at Trend Micro Email Security (`*.tmes.trendmicro.com`). Indicates Trend Micro's cloud email gateway handles inbound mail for the domain.
SPF `include:spf.tmes.trendmicro.com` authorizes Trend Micro Email Security to send mail on behalf of the domain. Typically paired with the Trend Micro MX.

Sophos domain-verification TXT (Sophos is an endpoint- and-network-security vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a Sophos Central tenant. Does not identify which Sophos product (Intercept X, Email, Firewall, MDR, and so on) is in use.

Palo Alto Networks domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Palo Alto tenant. Does not identify which Palo Alto product (Prisma Cloud, Cortex XDR, Cortex XSIAM, Strata firewalls, and so on) the tenant is licensed for.

Zscaler domain-verification TXT (Zscaler is a Secure Service Edge / SASE vendor providing ZIA and ZPA). Indicates the domain is registered with a Zscaler tenant. Does not distinguish ZIA (Internet Access) from ZPA (Private Access) deployments.

Catch-all TXT pattern containing the literal string "zscaler", for legacy or non-standard verification records. Lower specificity than the prefixed form; subject to false positives on TXT records that mention Zscaler in unrelated contexts.

Wiz domain-verification TXT (Wiz is a cloud-security- posture-management vendor, acquired by Google in 2025). Indicates the domain is registered with a Wiz tenant. Does not by itself indicate which cloud accounts the tenant covers (AWS, Azure, GCP, OCI) or whether scanning is currently active.

OneTrust domain-verification TXT (OneTrust is a privacy, consent-management, and GRC vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a OneTrust tenant. Does not identify which OneTrust module (Privacy, Consent, ESG, GRC, Vendor Risk) is in use.
Have I Been Pwned domain-verification TXT (Troy Hunt's breach-notification service). Indicates the domain owner has claimed domain-level breach monitoring on the service.

Duo Security SSO domain-verification TXT (Duo is a Cisco-owned MFA and zero-trust-access vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with Duo SSO, typically as part of a Single Sign-On deployment. Does not prove MFA is enforced on user accounts; the SSO tenant claim is administrative.

Okta domain-verification TXT (the `_oktaverification=` form). Indicates the domain is registered with an Okta tenant for use as a custom-domain login URL or for SCIM provisioning. Does not by itself indicate Okta is the primary IdP; some domains register with Okta as a secondary verification path.
Alternative Okta verification TXT format (`okta-domain-verification`). Same administrative-binding semantics as `_oktaverification=`.

CNAME pointing into okta.com. Typically a login subdomain (`login.example.com` or `sso.example.com`) delegated to Okta for hosted SSO and MFA pages. Indicates active Okta use for at least one identity flow.

CNAME pointing into oktapreview.com (Okta's preview / staging environment). Indicates the domain has a test or pre-production Okta tenant in addition to (or instead of) a production tenant. Common in environments with separate test orgs.

1Password domain-verification TXT (1Password is a password-manager and secrets-management vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a 1Password Business or Teams account, typically for SSO federation or domain-bound enrollment. Does not prove employee-wide rollout.

Jamf domain-verification TXT (Jamf is an Apple device management vendor, formerly JAMF Software). Indicates the domain is registered with a Jamf Pro or Jamf Now tenant. Does not indicate device-enrollment counts or MDM scope; the domain claim is administrative.

GlobalSign domain-control-validation TXT (the prefixed `_globalsign-domain-verification=` form, found at `_globalsign-domain-verification.<domain>`). Indicates the domain has been verified for issuance of a GlobalSign certificate at some point. The token may persist after the certificate has been replaced or retired; confidence is medium for that reason.
Alternative GlobalSign DCV TXT format (`globalsign-domain-verification` without the leading underscore). Same caveats as the underscored form: the token can persist after certificate replacement.

CNAME pointing into incapdns.net (Imperva Cloud WAF, formerly Incapsula). Indicates the apex or a subdomain sits behind Imperva's WAF and CDN edge. Does not distinguish Imperva's WAF tier (Essentials, Plus, Premier) on its own.
CNAME pointing into sucuri.net (Sucuri is a GoDaddy-owned website-security and WAF vendor). Indicates the domain sits behind Sucuri's WAF. Common with small business and WordPress hosting.

MX terminating at Symantec MessageLabs / Symantec Email Security.cloud (`*.messagelabs.com`). Now a Broadcom product after the Symantec Enterprise Security acquisition. Indicates Symantec MessageLabs sits in front of inbound mail.
SPF `include:spf.messagelabs.com` authorizes Symantec MessageLabs to send mail on behalf of the domain. Typically paired with the MessageLabs MX.

MX terminating at Cisco IronPort Hosted Email Security (`*.iphmx.com`). Indicates Cisco IronPort sits in front of inbound mail. Note: this is the older IronPort hosted-platform domain; new deployments often use the `ess.cisco.com` form covered by the `cisco-email` slug.

MX terminating at Cisco Secure Email Cloud Gateway (`*.ess.cisco.com`, formerly Email Security Service). Indicates Cisco Secure Email sits in front of inbound mail. This is the current Cisco-branded form of the service formerly sold under the IronPort name.

Cisco Webex domain-verification TXT. Indicates the domain is registered with a Webex tenant for SSO claim or custom-domain meetings. Does not prove active Webex usage; the verification is administrative. Webex is categorized here as "security" rather than "productivity" because Cisco's enterprise positioning bundles Webex with the broader Cisco security stack.

Cisco Common Identity domain-verification TXT (the "CI" stands for Cisco's Common Identity service, used across Webex, Cisco SecureX, and other Cisco SaaS). Indicates the domain is registered with Cisco's identity-platform tenant.
Alternative Cisco Common Identity verification TXT format (no hyphens). Same administrative-binding semantics as the hyphenated form.

MX terminating at Trellix Cloud Email Security (`*.fireeyecloud.com`, retaining the FireEye-era cloud-domain name). Trellix was formed in 2022 from the merger of McAfee Enterprise and FireEye. Indicates Trellix Email Security sits in front of inbound mail.
SentinelOne Singularity domain-verification TXT (SentinelOne is an EDR / XDR vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a SentinelOne tenant. Does not prove agent rollout on endpoints.

Netskope domain-verification TXT (Netskope is a SASE / SSE vendor offering CASB, SWG, and ZTNA). Indicates the domain is registered with a Netskope tenant. Does not distinguish which Netskope products are licensed.

Kandji domain-verification TXT (Kandji is an Apple device management vendor, comparable to Jamf for the Apple-only segment). Indicates the domain is registered with a Kandji tenant. Does not indicate enrollment scope.
PingOne domain-verification TXT (Ping Identity offers PingOne for cloud identity and PingFederate for hybrid deployments). Indicates the domain is registered with a PingOne tenant.
CNAME pointing into ping-ccd.com (Ping's Custom Cloud Domain service for hosted SSO and MFA pages). Indicates the domain delegates an authentication subdomain to PingOne.
PingOne email-trust verification TXT (`pingoneemail=` prefix). Authorizes PingOne to send email on behalf of the domain, typically for password-reset and passwordless-authentication flows.

CyberArk domain-verification TXT (CyberArk is a privileged-access-management vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a CyberArk tenant, typically for Identity or Privilege Cloud setup. Does not identify which CyberArk product is in use.

CNAME terminating in `idaptive.com` or `idaptive.app`, the legacy domain for CyberArk Identity (originally Idaptive, acquired by CyberArk in 2020). Indicates active CyberArk Identity portal use; the legacy domain is still in service after the rebrand.

Lakera domain-verification TXT (Lakera is an LLM- security and prompt-injection-defense vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a Lakera tenant. Does not prove Lakera Guard or Lakera Chrome is currently inline on application traffic.
Cato Networks domain-verification TXT (Cato is a SASE vendor offering an integrated SD-WAN, SWG, ZTNA, and FWaaS stack). Indicates the domain is registered with a Cato tenant.

CNAME pointing into auth0.com (Auth0 is an Okta-owned developer-focused IdP and CIAM vendor, acquired by Okta in 2021). Typically a `login.example.com` or `auth.example.com` delegation. Indicates active Auth0 use for at least one identity flow; distinct from the Okta Workforce or Customer Identity products even though both vendors share Okta as parent.
OneLogin domain-verification TXT (OneLogin is an IdP vendor, acquired by One Identity in 2021). Indicates the domain is registered with a OneLogin tenant.
CNAME pointing into onelogin.com, typically a login subdomain delegated to OneLogin for hosted SSO pages. Indicates active OneLogin use for at least one identity flow.
Snyk domain-verification TXT (Snyk is an SCA / SAST / container-scanning vendor). Indicates the domain is registered with a Snyk tenant, typically for SSO or web-asset-scanning authorization.

Alternative Snyk site-verification TXT format used for web-asset scanning authorization. Same administrative- binding semantics; web-asset scanning requires this verification to confirm ownership before scanning.

GitHub Enterprise domain-challenge TXT (the `_github-challenge:` form found at `_github-challenge.<domain>`). Indicates the domain has been verified to a GitHub Enterprise Cloud organization; a precondition for using GitHub Advanced Security features (code scanning, secret scanning, dependency review) on Enterprise repos under that domain.

Sonatype domain-verification TXT (Sonatype operates Nexus Repository, Nexus IQ, and Maven Central). Indicates the domain is registered with a Sonatype tenant for Nexus IQ or Nexus Lifecycle policy management.

Sonatype OSS Repository Hosting namespace-verification TXT (`OSSRH-<ticket>` form). Indicates the domain has been claimed for publishing artifacts to Maven Central under a reverse-domain group ID. The presence of this record is a precondition for publishing to Central, so finding it indicates the org publishes (or has published) Java or JVM artifacts under that domain's coordinates.

Cosign container-image-signing public key or verification TXT record. Indicates the domain publishes a Cosign public key (or verification record) for supply-chain attestation of container images. Confidence is medium because the convention is not standardized across the Sigstore ecosystem; some teams publish keys via other mechanisms.
Beyond Identity domain-verification TXT (Beyond Identity is a passwordless-MFA vendor using device-bound cryptographic keys). Indicates the domain is registered with a Beyond Identity tenant.

CNAME pointing into authenticator.beyondidentity.com, Beyond Identity's platform-authenticator endpoint. Indicates the domain delegates a passwordless-MFA authenticator subdomain to Beyond Identity. Confidence is medium because the CNAME may be present for pilot or partial rollouts.

CNAME terminating in workspaceoneaccess.com, VMware Workspace ONE Access (formerly VMware Identity Manager, acquired by Broadcom in 2023). Indicates the domain delegates an identity subdomain to Workspace ONE.

CNAME terminating in vmwareidentity.com, the legacy VMware Identity Manager domain that predates the Workspace ONE rebrand. Functionally equivalent to the `workspaceoneaccess.com` form; some tenants still use the legacy CNAME.

Subdomain CNAME terminating at a Shopify-hosted storefront (canonical form `<shop>.myshopify.com`). Indicates the org runs a Shopify storefront for at least one branded subdomain. Does not indicate Shopify Plus vs basic plan; the chain is identical across tiers.
