CNAME into Shopify's image and asset CDN (`*.shopifycdn.com`). Indicates the storefront serves product imagery through Shopify's hosted CDN. Does not by itself prove an active storefront; orphaned product images can persist after a shop is unpublished.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Zendesk help-center portal. Indicates the org runs a Zendesk hosted help center on a branded domain. Does not distinguish Zendesk Suite from Zendesk Sell; both products share the same custom-domain chain. Hosting-binding signal only, not a ticket-volume signal.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Thinkific-hosted course site (commonly a learn. or academy. subdomain). Indicates the org runs a Thinkific online-course / LMS storefront on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only, not a course-catalog or enrollment-volume signal.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Crowdin localization project. Suggests the org uses Crowdin for software / content translation management on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only, not a translation-volume or plan-tier signal.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to an Evervault Relay endpoint. Suggests the org routes traffic through Evervault for field-level encryption / tokenization. Hosting-binding signal only, not a data-volume signal.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to an Intellum-hosted learning platform. Suggests the org runs an Intellum LMS / customer-education site on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only, not an enrollment-volume signal.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Rocketlane customer-onboarding / professional-services-automation portal. Suggests the org runs Rocketlane for client onboarding and delivery on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Bettermode (formerly Tribe) community site. Suggests the org runs a Bettermode customer-community / forum on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only, not a membership-volume signal.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to an Impartner partner-relationship- management (PRM) portal. Suggests the org runs an Impartner partner / channel portal on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a LumApps employee-experience site. Suggests the org runs LumApps as a digital workplace / intranet on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Very Good Security (VGS) proxy route. Suggests the org routes traffic through VGS for tokenization / data redaction (PCI / PII). Hosting-binding signal only, not a data-volume signal.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a SecurityPal portal. Suggests the org uses SecurityPal for security-questionnaire / compliance automation on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Great Question research site. Suggests the org runs Great Question for user-research / customer-interview programs on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Marvin (Hey Marvin) research repository. Suggests the org uses Marvin for qualitative user-research analysis on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to an AirMason digital-handbook site. Suggests the org runs AirMason for employee handbooks / policy documents on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a NuData Security (Mastercard) endpoint. Suggests the org integrates NuData behavioral- biometrics / fraud-prevention. Hosting-binding signal only.

Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a ManageEngine (Zoho) IT-management product such as ServiceDesk Plus. Suggests the org runs ManageEngine for IT service / asset management on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Northflank-hosted application. Suggests the org deploys services on Northflank's developer platform (PaaS) on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a URLgenius deep-link domain. Suggests the org uses URLgenius for mobile deep linking / app attribution on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to an Extole referral / advocacy program. Suggests the org runs Extole for refer-a-friend / customer-advocacy marketing on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Staffbase employee-communications site. Suggests the org runs Staffbase as an internal-comms / intranet platform on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Superfiliate microsite. Suggests the org runs Superfiliate for creator / affiliate-commerce programs on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Transcend asset / consent endpoint. Suggests the org uses Transcend for data-privacy / consent management (GDPR / CCPA) on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.

Custom sync endpoint CNAMEd to Transcend's documented sync-transcend-cdn.com alias for consent synchronization. Same privacy / consent-management signal as the Transcend module endpoint, scoped to client-side sync traffic.

Custom-domain CNAME delegated to an Auth0 (Okta CIC) tenant for hosted login pages or universal-login flows. Indicates Auth0 is the org's customer-identity surface for at least one application. Does not prove enterprise-tier features (Organizations, custom databases); those are configured inside the Auth0 tenant and not visible from the chain.

Descope custom-domain CNAME target for hosted authentication pages on US projects. Indicates the org delegates at least one branded auth subdomain to Descope for customer identity flows. Hosting-binding signal only; it does not identify enabled auth methods or tenant policy.
Descope custom-domain CNAME target for hosted authentication pages on EU projects. Same identity-flow binding semantics as the US target, with regional Descope hosting.

Branded authentication subdomain terminating at Clerk's production Cloudflare worker namespace. Clerk documents a required Frontend API CNAME for production domains; this provider-specific target recurred across independent public namespaces. It indicates a Clerk-managed authentication surface, not which sign-in methods or policies are active.

Okta custom-domain delegation CNAME for hosted-SSO login at a branded subdomain. Indicates Okta is the org's primary or enterprise IdP. Does not distinguish Okta Workforce from Okta Customer Identity (Auth0); the chain is shared. Treat as a load-bearing identity-stack binding signal that is best corroborated by MX and federation metadata.
Subdomain CNAMEs into mailgun.org, typically a branded sending-domain or tracking subdomain on a Mailgun account. Indicates the domain uses Mailgun for transactional or bulk outbound email.

Subdomain CNAMEs into sendgrid.net, typically a branded sending-domain or click-tracking subdomain on a SendGrid (Twilio) account. Indicates the domain uses SendGrid for transactional or bulk outbound email.

CNAME into mandrillapp.com, Mailchimp Transactional (the rebranded Mandrill service). Indicates the org sends transactional mail through Mandrill rather than (or alongside) Mailchimp's marketing-mail surface. Does not indicate marketing-list volume; the two product paths are billed separately.
Subdomain CNAMEs into postmarkapp.com, typically a branded sending-domain on a Postmark (ActiveCampaign) account. Indicates the domain uses Postmark for transactional email.
Subdomain CNAMEs into pstmrk.com (Postmark's link-tracking shortened-redirect domain). Indicates Postmark click and open tracking under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into sparkpostmail.com, typically a branded sending-domain on a SparkPost (MessageBird / Bird) account. Indicates the domain uses SparkPost for transactional or marketing email.
Subdomain CNAMEs into klaviyo.com, typically a branded tracking or sending subdomain on a Klaviyo account. Common in e-commerce setups using Klaviyo for email and SMS marketing.
Subdomain CNAMEs into hubspotpagebuilder.com (HubSpot's hosted-landing-page domain). Indicates the domain serves at least one HubSpot landing page under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into hs-sites.com (HubSpot CMS site- hosting domain). Indicates the domain hosts pages (blog, knowledge base, marketing site) on HubSpot CMS.
Subdomain CNAMEs into hubspot.net (HubSpot's CDN and hosted-page endpoint). Indicates the domain serves HubSpot-hosted content under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into hsforms.net (HubSpot Forms hosting endpoint). Indicates the domain embeds or proxies HubSpot-hosted forms under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into intercom.help (Intercom's knowledge-base and help-center hosting endpoint). Indicates the domain serves a public Intercom help center under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into intercomcdn.com (Intercom's CDN for messenger assets and help-center static content). Indicates the domain proxies Intercom-hosted assets.
Subdomain CNAMEs into intercomhelpcenter.com (Intercom Help Center's alternate hosting endpoint). Same help-center hosting semantics as the intercom.help form; some tenants use this variant.
Subdomain CNAMEs into mktoresp.com (Marketo's response / tracking endpoint). Indicates the domain proxies Marketo response tracking through a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into marketo.com (Marketo, now Adobe Marketing Cloud after the 2018 acquisition). Indicates the domain proxies Marketo content or tracking through a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into mktoweb.com (Marketo's landing- page hosting endpoint). Indicates the domain hosts Marketo landing pages under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into mktossl.com (Marketo's SSL- enabled landing-page endpoint). Indicates HTTPS-served Marketo landing pages under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into Marketo tracking-link hosts such as ``mkto-[...]`` domains. Indicates branded marketing links or landing-page flows served through Adobe Marketo.

Subdomain CNAMEs into mktoapps.com (an Adobe Marketo Engage landing-page and microsite hosting backend, e.g. ``live.mktoapps.com``). Indicates Marketo-served pages under a branded subdomain; presence does not by itself confirm the Marketo tenant identity.
Subdomain CNAMEs into hs.eloqua.com (Oracle Eloqua's hosted-content endpoint). Indicates the domain proxies Eloqua forms, landing pages, or tracking through a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into en25.com (Oracle Eloqua's hosted landing-page domain; en25 is Eloqua's legacy product code). Indicates Eloqua-hosted landing pages under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into edge2.salesforce.com (Salesforce Experience Cloud edge endpoint). Indicates Salesforce Experience Cloud (formerly Communities) site hosting under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into force.com (Salesforce's developer-platform domain). Indicates the domain hosts Salesforce-built apps (Force.com / Lightning Platform) under a branded subdomain.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Salesforce's `siteforce.com` namespace, including the documented `live.siteforce.com` custom-domain targets. Indicates Salesforce Sites or Experience Cloud hosting under a branded subdomain; it does not identify the site's audience or whether authentication is required.
Subdomain CNAMEs into fe.pardot.com (Pardot's frontend hosting endpoint, now Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement). Indicates the domain hosts Pardot landing pages or forms.
Subdomain CNAMEs into pardot.com (Pardot is Salesforce's B2B marketing-automation product, rebranded to Marketing Cloud Account Engagement). Indicates Pardot-hosted content under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into campaign.adobe.com (Adobe Campaign hosted endpoint). Indicates the domain uses Adobe Campaign for marketing automation.
Subdomain CNAMEs into cjm.adobe.com (Adobe Customer Journey Management). Indicates the domain uses Adobe's CJM offering, part of Adobe Experience Cloud.

Subdomain CNAMEs into service-now.com (ServiceNow's tenant-hosting domain). Indicates the domain has a branded subdomain pointing to a ServiceNow instance (typically `now.example.com` or `servicenow.example.com`). Common with ITSM, ITOM, and enterprise-workflow deployments.
Subdomain CNAMEs into wixsite.com (Wix's hosted-site endpoint). Indicates the domain serves a Wix-built site under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into wixdns.net (Wix's DNS hosting domain). Indicates the domain uses Wix-managed DNS, typically paired with Wix site hosting.

Bitly branded short-link domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs a custom short-link domain (e.g. brand.ly) through Bitly's hosted shortener. Does not prove enterprise-tier adoption; Bitly Custom Domains is available on multiple tiers and the CNAME is identical.
Subdomain CNAMEs into cname.short.io (Short.io is a branded-URL-shortener service comparable to Bitly). Indicates the domain operates a branded short-link service through Short.io.

Better Stack (formerly Better Uptime) hosted status-page endpoint CNAME. Indicates the org publishes a public status page through Better Stack. Does not prove the org's incident-response process runs through Better Stack; the status page can be a passive presence.

Waiting-room CNAME target under Queue-it's queue-it.net service domain. Indicates the subdomain routes end users through Queue-it's virtual waiting-room flow. Hosting-binding signal only; it does not identify protected paths, queue configuration, or event volume.
Subdomain CNAMEs into unbouncepages.com (Unbounce's hosted landing-page endpoint). Indicates the domain serves Unbounce-built landing pages under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into tm-3.office.com (Microsoft 365 traffic-manager office.com zone, used for Autodiscover and Teams routing). Indicates the domain delegates an M365 endpoint subdomain.

Subdomain CNAMEs into sharepoint.com (the tenant-name.sharepoint.com pattern is the default domain for SharePoint Online tenants). Indicates the domain delegates a SharePoint-branded subdomain to Microsoft 365.
Subdomain CNAMEs into wpenginepowered.com (WP Engine's hosted-WordPress endpoint domain). Indicates the domain hosts a WordPress site on WP Engine infrastructure.
Subdomain CNAMEs into netlifyglobalcdn.com (Netlify's global edge endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates a Netlify-deployed site to Netlify's CDN.

Subdomain CNAMEs into b-cdn.net (Bunny CDN's edge endpoint, sometimes called bunny.net). Indicates the domain delegates to Bunny CDN, a cost-focused CDN popular in cost-sensitive media and download workloads.

WordPress VIP (Automattic) managed-hosting CNAME for enterprise WordPress workloads. Indicates the org is on the VIP managed tier rather than self-hosted WordPress or WordPress.com. Does not by itself indicate the editorial scale; VIP serves both single-site and multi-site newsrooms.

WorkOS B2B SSO custom-domain CNAME for hosted SSO-as-a-service. Indicates the org embeds WorkOS as its B2B-SSO layer in front of customer-facing apps. Does not by itself indicate SCIM or directory-sync adoption; those are separate WorkOS products on the same tenant.
Alternative WorkOS custom-domain CNAME using the hyphenated zone (newer rollouts). Functionally equivalent to the `workosdns.com` form; some tenants run on the newer zone.

PingOne identity-as-a-service tenant CNAME for hosted SSO and MFA pages. Indicates Ping is the org's IdP or customer-identity surface for at least one application. Does not distinguish PingOne for Workforce from PingOne for Customers; the chain is shared across the PingOne product family.

Salesloft custom-tracking-subdomain CNAME for sales engagement (Cadence, Conversations). Indicates the org runs Salesloft as part of its sales-engagement stack. Does not by itself indicate Drift adoption; Drift is a separate Salesloft product line.

Pendo product-analytics customer-endpoint CNAME. Indicates the org runs Pendo on at least one product surface for in-app analytics and guides. Does not by itself indicate the integration depth; Pendo can be deployed as a single Snippet or as a full Engage rollout.

Docebo learning-management-system tenant CNAME. Indicates the org runs Docebo as its LMS for at least one branded learning subdomain. Does not indicate audience (employee vs customer training); Docebo serves both topologies on the same chain.

Skilljar customer-education-platform CNAME. Indicates the org runs Skilljar as its customer-training LMS. Does not by itself indicate course volume; a single course produces the same chain as a multi-course catalog.

Bizzabo events-platform custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org has stood up at least one branded event hub on Bizzabo. Does not by itself indicate event cadence; the chain persists between events independent of activity.

Instatus hosted status-page CNAME (a lower-cost competitor to Statuspage and Better Stack). Indicates the org publishes a status page through Instatus. Does not indicate paid-tier adoption; Instatus offers free tier on the same chain.

Frontify brand-asset-management custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs a branded Frontify portal for design-system or brand-guideline distribution. Does not prove the portal is public-facing; many Frontify tenants sit behind invitation-only auth.

ReadMe documentation-hub custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org publishes developer docs through ReadMe on a branded subdomain. Does not by itself indicate API audience size; ReadMe serves both internal and public doc surfaces on the same chain.

WP Engine WordPress-hosting proxy CNAME (variant zone). Indicates the org is on WP Engine's managed-WordPress platform via the alternative proxy chain. Functionally equivalent to the `wpengine.com` chain; some tenants run on this variant.

Swoogo events-platform custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs Swoogo for event-registration sites. Does not by itself indicate event scale; corporate single-event use produces the same chain as professional-association annual-conference use.

UptimeRobot hosted public-status-page CNAME. Indicates the org publishes uptime data through UptimeRobot. Does not by itself indicate paid-tier adoption; free-tier status pages produce the same chain.

Uptime.com hosted status-page custom-domain CNAME (points at `cname.uptime.com`). Indicates the org publishes a public status page through Uptime.com. Distinct from UptimeRobot; does not by itself indicate the monitoring plan tier.

Subdomain CNAME terminating at an ngrok custom-domain edge (`*.ngrok-cname.com`). Indicates the org fronts a service through an ngrok ingress endpoint. Does not by itself reveal the upstream service or whether the tunnel is currently active.

Subdomain CNAME terminating in ngrok's legacy or regional `ngrok.io` endpoint namespace. Indicates routing through an ngrok ingress endpoint. It does not reveal the upstream service or prove that a tunnel is currently active.

bl.ink branded URL-shortener custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs a branded short-link domain through bl.ink (a Bitly competitor). Does not by itself indicate marketing-attribution depth; the chain only shows the hosted-shortener binding.

GoDaddy Workspace Email hosted-endpoint CNAME. Indicates the org's email runs through GoDaddy's hosted-mail service (a smaller-business tier, comparable to Microsoft 365 Business Basic). Does not by itself indicate the size of the email footprint.

cloud.gov FedRAMP-authorized PaaS endpoint CNAME for US federal agency workloads. Indicates the agency runs production workloads on cloud.gov. Does not by itself indicate FedRAMP impact level; cloud.gov offers Moderate and the chain is shared across agency tenants.

Pantheon managed-hosting edge endpoint CNAME for Drupal and WordPress workloads. Indicates the org runs Pantheon for at least one branded site. Does not distinguish Pantheon's Elite (enterprise) tier from standard; the chain is shared.

Symphony Talent / SAP SuccessFactors recruiting-marketing custom-domain CNAME (jobs2web is the legacy product name after the SuccessFactors acquisition). Indicates the org runs Symphony Talent for candidate-experience pages. Functionally equivalent to direct SuccessFactors recruiting in most deployments.

Presspage PR / newsroom custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org publishes press releases through Presspage on a branded subdomain. Does not by itself indicate press-cadence; a single-release setup produces the same chain as an active newsroom.

Localist event-calendar-platform CNAME for higher-ed and institutional event hubs. Indicates the org runs Localist as its public event-calendar surface. Does not by itself indicate event volume; the chain reflects platform binding only.

RainFocus event-management-platform custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs RainFocus for one or more branded event hubs. Does not by itself indicate event cadence; the chain persists between events.

Squarespace external-customer custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org's website is hosted by Squarespace on a branded domain. Does not by itself distinguish Squarespace Personal, Business, or Commerce tiers; the chain is identical.

Squarespace-managed subdomain CNAME for manually adding a subdomain to a Squarespace site. Indicates the branded subdomain points to Squarespace-hosted site content, not a specific plan tier or account relationship.

HubSpot CMS EU CDN zone for content delivered through HubSpot's European edge infrastructure. Indicates the org runs HubSpot CMS with EU-region content hosting. Does not by itself indicate GDPR posture; data-residency controls are configured separately inside the HubSpot tenant.

HubSpot CMS North America CDN zone for content delivered through HubSpot's NA edge infrastructure. Same product family as hscoscdn-eu1.net; the suffix difference reflects the tenant's hosting region. Does not distinguish HubSpot CMS tier (Starter, Professional, Enterprise); the chain is shared.

Akamai Edge staging-zone CNAME. Indicates the org has an Akamai staging environment in addition to a production deployment. Does not by itself prove production volume; staging is the testbed surface, not the customer-traffic surface.

Subdomain CNAMEs into cloudapp.azure.com (Azure's regional cloud-app endpoint domain, used by VMs, public load balancers, and Azure Cloud Services / VM web roles directly hosted on Azure compute). Lower-specificity infrastructure tier; identifies an Azure-hosted endpoint without distinguishing which Azure product.
Subdomain CNAMEs into AWS API Gateway in us-east-1. Indicates the domain delegates a branded API endpoint to an API Gateway deployment in N. Virginia.
Subdomain CNAMEs into AWS API Gateway in us-west-2 (Oregon). Indicates a branded API endpoint hosted in that region.
Subdomain CNAMEs into AWS API Gateway in eu-west-1 (Ireland). Indicates a branded API endpoint hosted in that region.
Subdomain CNAMEs into AWS API Gateway in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt). Indicates a branded API endpoint hosted in that region, common with EU-data-residency setups.
Subdomain CNAMEs into AWS API Gateway in ap-southeast-1 (Singapore). Indicates a branded API endpoint hosted in that region.
Subdomain CNAMEs into a regional AWS ALB or NLB endpoint in us-east-1. The slug uses "nlb" but the underlying ELB hostname covers both Application and Network Load Balancers.
Subdomain CNAMEs into a regional AWS ALB or NLB endpoint in us-west-2 (Oregon). Does not distinguish ALB from NLB; both produce this chain. Hosting-binding signal only, not a traffic-volume signal.
Subdomain CNAMEs into a regional AWS ALB or NLB endpoint in eu-west-1 (Ireland).
Subdomain CNAMEs into a regional AWS ALB or NLB endpoint in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt).
Subdomain CNAMEs into a regional AWS ALB or NLB endpoint in ap-southeast-1 (Singapore).

Subdomain CNAMEs into a regional AWS ALB or NLB endpoint in the us-gov-east-1 AWS GovCloud (US-East) region. Suggests the org runs workloads in AWS GovCloud, the partition for US government and regulated workloads. Does not distinguish ALB from NLB, and does not by itself confirm a compliance boundary.
Subdomain CNAMEs into a regional AWS ALB or NLB endpoint in the us-gov-west-1 AWS GovCloud (US-West) region. Same GovCloud partition as us-gov-east-1. Does not distinguish ALB from NLB.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Reblaze (part of Link11), a cloud WAAP that fronts the origin with WAF, DDoS, and bot mitigation. Indicates traffic to the subdomain is proxied through Reblaze. Marks the protective front, not the origin stack behind it.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Indusface AppTrana (indusguard.com), a managed WAAP that proxies the site for WAF, DDoS, and bot protection. Indicates traffic is routed through AppTrana. Marks the protective front, not the origin behind it.
Subdomain CNAMEs into the Indusface AppTrana CDN layer (induscdn.com), the content-delivery tier paired with the AppTrana WAAP. Indicates AppTrana fronts the subdomain. Hosting-binding signal only.

Subdomain CNAMEs into SharpSpring (now Constant Contact Lead Gen and CRM) on marketingautomation.services, used for hosted landing pages and tracked email links. Suggests the org runs SharpSpring for marketing automation on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.

Subdomain CNAMEs into HugeDomains, a domain reseller and parking host. Indicates the subdomain is parked or listed for sale rather than serving an active application. A hygiene signal that the label resolves but is not in productive use.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Microsoft Entra Application Proxy (msappproxy.net), which publishes an on-premises web app for remote access through Entra ID. Indicates the org exposes an internal application via Entra, not that the app itself runs in Azure. Does not name the specific on-premises application.

Subdomain CNAMEs into the Workato API platform (apim.workato.com), the default host for Workato-managed API endpoints. Suggests the org runs Workato for integration and automation (iPaaS) and fronts its APIs on a branded domain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into Workato API-platform custom-domain hosting (apim-custom.workato.com). Same iPaaS attribution as apim.workato.com, on a customer custom domain. Hosting-binding signal only.

Subdomain CNAMEs into ByteArk, a Thailand-based CDN and video- streaming provider. Indicates content for the subdomain is delivered through ByteArk's CDN. Hosting-binding signal only, not a traffic-volume signal.

Subdomain CNAMEs into SiteDetour, a hosted URL-redirect and dynamic-QR platform. Indicates the subdomain serves redirects rather than an origin application. A hosting-binding signal for the redirect tier, not the destination it points at.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Claranet (clara.net), a managed-services and hosting provider. Indicates Claranet hosts or fronts the subdomain. Does not distinguish managed hosting from the other services Claranet runs on the same domain.

Subdomain CNAMEs into BAMKO (bamkounified.com), a branded- merchandise vendor that hosts per-customer company stores. Suggests the org runs a BAMKO-hosted swag or company store on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Gamania CloudForce (cloudforce.gamania.com), a Taiwan-based CDN, DNS, and anti-DDoS provider. Indicates content for the subdomain is delivered or fronted through Gamania CloudForce. Hosting-binding signal only, common on Taiwan-region properties.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Turbify (turbify.biz, formerly Yahoo Small Business), a small-business shared web-hosting provider. Indicates the subdomain is hosted on Turbify cPanel hosting. Common on small-business and long-tail sites.

Paradox.ai careers-chatbot (Olivia) custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs Paradox.ai as its conversational recruiting layer. Does not prove application volume; the chain is the platform-binding signal, not the candidate-funnel signal.

Jibe / iCIMS Apply careers-landing-page CNAME (jibe is now the iCIMS Talent Cloud careers product). Indicates the org uses iCIMS for candidate-experience pages. Functionally equivalent to direct iCIMS chain rules.

Career.page hosted careers-site CNAME (a lightweight alternative to Greenhouse, Lever, and similar ATS-hosted careers pages). Indicates the org runs a basic careers page on Career.page rather than a full ATS-hosted surface.
Happydance careers-platform custom-domain CNAME (a Workday-adjacent careers-page builder). Indicates the org runs Happydance for candidate-facing pages, typically on top of Workday Recruiting.

EasyRedir managed-URL-redirects CNAME. Indicates the org runs EasyRedir for hosted URL redirects (mergers, rebrands, vanity domains). Does not by itself indicate redirect volume; the chain is the platform-binding signal only.

SAP Customer Data Cloud (formerly Gigya) hosted-endpoint CNAME. Indicates the org runs SAP CDC for customer identity and consent management. Functionally equivalent to direct SAP CDC chain rules; Gigya is the legacy product name.

F5 Distributed Cloud (formerly Volterra) custom-domain delegation CNAME. Indicates the org runs F5 XC for multi-cloud networking, WAF, or app-delivery. Does not distinguish the specific F5 XC product; the chain is shared across the F5 XC service family.

Radware Cloud WAF and DDoS-protection custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org routes at least one application through Radware's cloud-protection edge. Does not by itself indicate the policy posture; rule sets are configured inside the Radware tenant.

ForgeRock Identity Cloud tenant CNAME (ForgeRock is now a Ping Identity company after the 2023 acquisition). Indicates the org runs ForgeRock for identity and access management. Functionally equivalent to direct Ping Identity chains in some post-acquisition setups.

IO River multi-CDN-edge-platform CNAME. Indicates the org runs IO River as a CDN-management abstraction across multiple underlying CDNs. Does not reveal which underlying CDNs are in the IO River configuration.

Section.io edge-compute / CDN-endpoint CNAME. Indicates the org runs Section.io for edge-deployed workloads. Does not by itself indicate the workload type; Section.io hosts a wide range of edge applications on the same chain.
Azion edge-compute-platform CNAME (Azion is a South-America-focused edge-compute alternative to Cloudflare and Fastly). Indicates the org runs Azion for at least one edge-deployed workload.
Acquia Cloud Drupal hosting CNAME. Indicates the org runs managed-Drupal through Acquia. Does not distinguish Acquia Site Factory (multi-site) from single-site Acquia Cloud; the chain is shared.
Pagely managed-WordPress hosting CNAME (enterprise-tier WordPress hosting, comparable to WP Engine Premium and WordPress VIP). Indicates the org runs Pagely as a managed-WordPress provider.

Outreach sales-engagement custom-subdomain CNAME for the user-facing portal. Indicates the org runs Outreach as its sales-engagement layer. Does not by itself indicate seat count; the chain reflects platform binding only.

Zuddl events-platform whitelabel custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs Zuddl for branded virtual or hybrid event hubs. Does not by itself indicate event cadence; the chain persists between events.
Postman hosted-status-page CNAME. Indicates the org publishes a Postman-hosted public status page. Does not by itself indicate API audience or partner count; the chain reflects platform binding only.

Site24x7 hosted public-status-pages CNAME (Site24x7 is a ManageEngine / Zoho product). Indicates the org publishes a public status page through Site24x7. Functionally equivalent to Statuspage or Better Stack for the status-page surface.

Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC) ExactTarget legacy zone CNAME (s7, s10, s11, and other tenant-instance zones). Indicates the org runs SFMC. Functionally equivalent to direct `marketingcloudapis.com` chains; ExactTarget is the pre-2013-acquisition product name.

SFMC content-delivery zone CNAME (cp-sap and related subzones). Indicates the org runs Marketing Cloud and delivers content through SFMC's CDN layer. Does not distinguish SFMC editions; the chain is shared across Enterprise, Pro, and Corporate tiers.

AWS S3 static-website-hosting endpoint CNAME (region- agnostic substring match). Indicates the org runs at least one static site hosted directly from an S3 bucket. Does not by itself indicate the bucket policy or whether CloudFront fronts the bucket.

AWS EC2 us-east-1 default-zone CNAME (legacy DNS form from the original EC2 region). Indicates the org runs an EC2 workload reachable via the legacy zone format. Functionally equivalent to the newer per-region `compute.amazonaws.com` form.

AWS EC2 regional-zone CNAME (the modern per-region form). Indicates the org runs an EC2 workload directly reachable via DNS. Does not by itself indicate the workload type; EC2 hosts arbitrary user workloads.

Microsoft 365 China (operated by 21Vianet) sovereign-cloud zone CNAME. Indicates the org runs Microsoft 365 on the 21Vianet-operated China sovereign tenant. Distinct from commercial M365; data residency and feature parity differ.

Webflow proxy-ssl-geo custom-domain CNAME (the SSL and geo-routing front for Webflow custom domains). Indicates the org runs Webflow for at least one branded site. Does not distinguish Webflow CMS tiers; the chain is shared across plans.

PagerDuty internal status / incident-page CNAME. Indicates the org uses PagerDuty's hosted incident- communication surface. Does not by itself indicate the org's on-call topology; the chain reflects platform binding only.

Fly.io app-platform-endpoint CNAME for hosted workloads on Fly's global edge. Indicates the org runs at least one workload on Fly. Does not distinguish Fly's Free, Hobby, and Launch tiers; the chain is shared.

Beehiiv newsletter-platform branded CNAME. Indicates the org runs a Beehiiv newsletter on a branded domain. Does not by itself indicate subscriber count; Beehiiv serves both early-stage and enterprise newsletters on the same chain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into sfmc-marketing.com (Salesforce Marketing Cloud link-tracking and click-attribution endpoint). Indicates the domain operates a tracked SFMC subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into marketingcloudapis.com (SFMC's API endpoint domain). Indicates the domain proxies Marketing Cloud API traffic through a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into exct.net (a legacy ExactTarget / SFMC tracking-link domain). Functionally equivalent to the sfmc-marketing.com endpoint; some tenants still use the shorter legacy form.
Subdomain CNAMEs into statuspage.io (Atlassian Statuspage hosted-status-page endpoint). Indicates the domain operates a public status page under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into statuspageio.com (alternative Atlassian Statuspage endpoint domain). Same hosted- status-page semantics as statuspage.io.

Subdomain CNAMEs into stspg-customer.com (Atlassian Statuspage customer-CNAME endpoint, used for `status.example.com` delegations). Same hosted- status-page semantics; this is the variant Statuspage directs new customers to use.

Subdomain CNAMEs into atlassian.net (Atlassian Cloud tenant domain, used for `tenant.atlassian.net` Jira / Confluence URLs). Indicates the domain delegates a branded subdomain to an Atlassian Cloud instance.
Subdomain CNAMEs into saas.atlassian.com (Atlassian- hosted status pages and other Atlassian SaaS endpoints). Indicates the domain uses an Atlassian- hosted SaaS surface.
Subdomain CNAMEs into pagerduty.com (PagerDuty's incident-management platform). Indicates the domain delegates a branded incident-response or on-call subdomain to PagerDuty.
Subdomain CNAMEs into docusign.net (DocuSign's hosted- signature endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates a branded signing-experience subdomain to DocuSign, typically for embedded-signing flows.
Subdomain CNAMEs into cloudinary.com (Cloudinary image / video CDN and processing). Indicates the domain proxies media assets through Cloudinary under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into stripecdn.com (Stripe's hosted checkout and dashboard CDN). Indicates the domain proxies Stripe checkout, customer portal, or related assets under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into apigee.net (Google Cloud Apigee API management). Indicates the domain proxies a branded API gateway endpoint through Apigee.
Subdomain CNAMEs into apigee.com (alternative Apigee tenant domain). Same API-management semantics as the apigee.net form.

Subdomain CNAMEs into anypointdns.net (MuleSoft Anypoint Platform's DNS-hosting endpoint, MuleSoft is a Salesforce property). Indicates the domain delegates a branded API or integration endpoint to MuleSoft.
Subdomain CNAMEs into submittable.com (Submittable is a submission-management platform for grants, scholarships, and content submissions). Indicates the domain operates a branded submission portal.
Subdomain CNAMEs into loopreturns.com (Loop is a returns-management platform for e-commerce, focused on Shopify). Indicates the domain operates a branded returns-portal subdomain.

Subdomain CNAMEs into attentivemobile.com (Attentive is an SMS and email marketing platform popular in e-commerce). Indicates the domain operates a branded Attentive subdomain for SMS opt-in flows or marketing campaigns.
Subdomain CNAMEs into attntags.com (Attentive's link-tracking and short-link domain). Indicates Attentive click and SMS-link tracking under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into iterable.com (Iterable is a cross-channel marketing automation platform). Indicates the domain proxies Iterable tracking or link-rewrites under a branded subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into customer.io (Customer.io is a behavioral-messaging platform). Indicates the domain operates a branded Customer.io tracking or link subdomain.

Branch.io mobile-deep-linking custom-domain CNAME. Indicates the org runs Branch for deferred deep links or attribution. Does not by itself indicate which Branch product is active (Linking, Attribution, Web-to-App).
Subdomain CNAMEs into herokudns.com (Heroku's custom-domain CNAME endpoint, used for SSL-enabled apps). Indicates the domain delegates an app subdomain to a Heroku dyno or formation.
Subdomain CNAMEs into herokuapp.com (Heroku's default-app hostname domain). Indicates the domain delegates to a Heroku-hosted application.
Subdomain CNAMEs into github.io (GitHub Pages static-site hosting). Indicates the domain serves a GitHub-hosted static site under a custom subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into webflow.io (Webflow's default hosted-site domain). Indicates the domain serves a Webflow-built site under a custom subdomain.
Subdomain CNAMEs into cdn.webflow.com (Webflow's CDN for static assets). Indicates the domain proxies Webflow-hosted assets through the Webflow CDN.
Subdomain CNAMEs into cname.vercel-dns.com (Vercel's custom-domain CNAME-pointer for non-apex hostnames). Indicates the domain delegates a branded subdomain to a Vercel-deployed application.
Subdomain CNAMEs into vercel.app (Vercel's default hosted-app hostname). Indicates the domain delegates a branded subdomain to a Vercel-deployed app.
Subdomain CNAMEs into a vercel-dns-* pattern (Vercel DNS verification or apex-helper endpoints). Indicates the domain uses Vercel-managed DNS for a custom-domain hookup.
Subdomain CNAMEs into netlify.app (Netlify's default hosted-site hostname). Indicates the domain delegates a branded subdomain to a Netlify-deployed site.
Subdomain CNAMEs into squarespace-cdn.com (Squarespace's CDN for hosted-site assets and images). Indicates the domain proxies Squarespace- hosted media through a branded subdomain.

Subdomain CNAMEs into crs.ultradns.net, the UltraDNS Web Forwarding endpoint for redirect records. Indicates the subdomain is configured as an UltraDNS-managed web forward, not that the destination site is hosted by UltraDNS.
Subdomain CNAMEs into fastly.net (Fastly CDN edge). Indicates the domain proxies traffic through Fastly's edge for CDN, image optimization, or Compute@Edge.
Subdomain CNAMEs into fastlylb.net (Fastly's load- balancer endpoint domain). Indicates the domain uses Fastly for load balancing in addition to CDN.

Subdomain CNAME terminating at CDN77's documented resource endpoint (`<resource-id>.rsc.cdn77.org`). Indicates delivery through CDN77's edge. It does not identify the origin, cache policy, or traffic volume.
Subdomain CNAMEs into cloudfront.net (AWS CloudFront CDN distribution). Indicates the domain proxies traffic through a CloudFront distribution, commonly in front of S3, API Gateway, or ELB origins.
Subdomain CNAMEs into elb.amazonaws.com (AWS Classic Load Balancer or unscoped regional ELB endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates to an AWS load balancer fronting EC2, ECS, or EKS workloads.

Subdomain CNAMEs into awsglobalaccelerator.com (AWS Global Accelerator network-layer endpoint). Indicates the domain uses Global Accelerator for cross-region traffic routing, often in front of multi-region AWS deployments.
Subdomain CNAMEs into awsapprunner.com (AWS App Runner managed-container endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates to an App Runner service, AWS's managed container-deployment offering.
Subdomain CNAMEs into elasticbeanstalk.com (AWS Elastic Beanstalk PaaS endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates to a Beanstalk environment, typically a multi-tier application managed by Beanstalk.
Subdomain CNAMEs into akamaiedge.net (Akamai edge CDN endpoint). Indicates the domain proxies through Akamai's edge for CDN or WAF services.
Subdomain CNAMEs into akamaized.net (Akamai's object-delivery and storage endpoint domain). Indicates the domain delegates static-asset delivery to Akamai.
Subdomain CNAMEs into edgekey.net (Akamai's edge-key product domain). Same edge-proxying semantics as akamaiedge.net.
Subdomain CNAMEs into edgesuite.net (Akamai EdgeSuite CDN and WAF product). Same edge-proxying semantics as the other Akamai edge domains.
Subdomain CNAMEs into akadns.net (Akamai Edge DNS or Global Traffic Management product). Indicates the domain uses Akamai-managed DNS or DNS-based traffic steering.
Subdomain CNAMEs into cloudflare.net (Cloudflare's edge-product endpoint domain). Indicates the domain proxies through Cloudflare's edge for CDN, WAF, or other Cloudflare services.
Subdomain CNAMEs into pages.dev (Cloudflare Pages hosted-site endpoint). Indicates the domain delegates a static-site or Jamstack deployment to Cloudflare Pages.
Subdomain CNAMEs into incapdns.net (Imperva Cloud WAF and DDoS-protection edge, formerly Incapsula). Indicates the domain proxies through Imperva's edge.
Subdomain CNAMEs into impervadns.net (Imperva's newer DNS-zone variant). Same WAF and DDoS-protection semantics as incapdns.net.
Subdomain CNAMEs into azureedge.net (Azure CDN edge, across Standard from Microsoft and the deprecating Akamai and Verizon partner tiers). Indicates the domain delegates to an Azure CDN profile.
Subdomain CNAMEs into azurefd.net (Azure Front Door global-edge endpoint). Indicates the domain proxies through Azure Front Door, Microsoft's global load balancer and edge-acceleration product.
Subdomain CNAMEs into trafficmanager.net (Azure Traffic Manager DNS-based traffic routing). Indicates the domain uses Traffic Manager for DNS-level failover or geographic routing.

Subdomain CNAMEs into ghs.googlehosted.com (Google Hosted Services endpoint, used for Google Sites and Google Workspace web-app delegation). Indicates the domain delegates a branded subdomain to a Google Workspace-hosted resource.

Google Workspace Sites / module CNAME on the newer `ghs.google.com` zone (the legacy form is `ghs.googlehosted.com`). Indicates the domain delegates a Google-hosted module surface. Does not by itself prove the org's email runs through Workspace; many verifications persist without the email tier.
Subdomain CNAMEs into sucuri.net (Sucuri is a GoDaddy- owned website-security and WAF vendor). Indicates the domain proxies through Sucuri's edge. Common with small-business and WordPress sites.

Microsoft 365 cloud autodiscover / service-zone CNAME (the newer Mira service surface). Indicates the org runs Microsoft 365 with the modern service-zone endpoints. Functionally equivalent in product binding to the legacy `*.outlook.com` autodiscover form.
Akamai Enterprise Application Access zero-trust network access; protected apps CNAME to *.go.akamai-access.com.
Amplience headless CMS / DAM; media CDN CNAMEs chain through cdn.media.amplience.net to dm.amplience.net.
BigMarker webinar / virtual events platform; custom event domains CNAME to whitelabel.bigmarker.com.
CampusPress managed WordPress hosting for universities; academic blog CNAMEs resolve to *.lb.campuspress.com.
Certain event management platform (acquired by Cvent); event subdomains chain through *.certain.com to cloud1.certainaws.com.

easyDNS registrar URL-forwarding-service CNAME (HTTP variant). Indicates the org uses easyDNS for hosted URL redirects. Does not by itself indicate registrar binding; URL forwarding can be enabled independently of where DNS is hosted.
easyDNS registrar URL-forwarding-service CNAME (HTTPS variant). Functionally equivalent to the HTTP variant but terminates the SSL handshake at the easyDNS edge.
Bitban EdgeCDN Spanish content delivery network; CNAMEs chain through edge2befaster.io to *.usg.edgetcdn.io.
Edgio (formerly Limelight Networks) CDN; customer media CNAMEs terminate at *.vo.llnwd.net.
Fanatics white-label sports merchandise platform; team-shop CNAMEs resolve through *.fanaticsretailgroup.com.
Antidot Fluid Topics cloud documentation portal; customer docs domains CNAME through *.fluidtopics.net to aws.antidot.net.
Fortinet FortiWeb Cloud WAF-as-a-Service; customer CNAMEs route through *.fortiwebcloud.net.

FortiWeb Cloud upstream backend CNAME (chains terminate at `aws.waas-online.net` and similar). Indicates the org routes at least one application through FortiWeb Cloud WAF. Does not by itself indicate FortiGate firewall adoption; FortiWeb is a separate Fortinet product.

Framer no-code website-builder CNAME (custom domains CNAME to `sites.framer.app`). Indicates the org runs a Framer-hosted site on a branded domain. Does not by itself indicate plan tier; Framer's free and paid tiers share the chain.
Gandi registrar web-redirect service; CNAMEs terminate at webredir.partners.gandi.net.
Gandi web-redirect (legacy zone); same forwarding service as partners.gandi.net.
Gatsby virtual / hybrid events platform; custom event domains CNAME to *.customers.gatsby.events.
GCP-hosted workloads with public IPs in the bc.googleusercontent.com reverse zone.
GitBook documentation platform; custom docs domains CNAME through hosting.gitbook.com to hosting.gitbook.io.
Hostinger hosted email service; autodiscover CNAMEs terminate at autodiscover.mail.hostinger.com.
IONOS (1&1) web hosting redirect / autodiscover endpoint; signals IONOS-hosted domains.
Kinsta managed WordPress hosting; customer sites CNAME to *.hosting.kinsta.cloud.
Lumen / CenturyLink (formerly Level 3) CDN edge network; customer CNAMEs terminate at *.c.footprint.net.
Medianova Turkish content delivery network; customer CDN CNAMEs terminate at *.sm.mncdn.com.
Merlin CDN Turkish content delivery network; customer CNAMEs resolve through *.merlincdn.net edge nodes.
Mintlify developer documentation platform; custom docs domains CNAME to cname.mintlify.builders.
Movable Ink email content personalization platform; image CNAMEs resolve through img.ed4.net to instantconfig.ed4.net.
Okta Government Cloud (FedRAMP); federal-agency custom logins CNAME through customdomains.okta-gov.com.

Okta DNSSEC-enabled custom-domain endpoint CNAME. Indicates the org runs Okta with DNSSEC turned on for the custom-domain surface (a hardening choice). Does not by itself indicate the broader DNSSEC posture for the org's apex.
Ping Identity PingFederate cloud; enterprise SSO / federation CNAMEs route through *.ping.cloud cluster endpoints.
Prowly PR / media newsroom platform; custom newsroom domains CNAME to cname.prowly.com.
Q4 Inc. investor relations website platform; IR subdomain CNAMEs terminate at webfarm-*.q4web.com.
Rackspace Email hosted email service; autodiscover CNAME to autodiscover.emailsrvr.com.
Refined Confluence / Jira theme platform with custom domain hosting; knowledge-base CNAMEs terminate at custom.domain.refined.site.
SAP Commerce Cloud (formerly Hybris) SaaS storefront; custom shop domains CNAME to *.cc.commerce.ondemand.com.
Stova (formerly Aventri) event management platform; custom event domains CNAME to *.micros.aventri.com.
Talentera MENA applicant tracking and career portal; careers subdomains CNAME to regional endpoints like ksa.talentera.com.
Terminus / Sigstr email signature marketing platform; customer subdomains CNAME to spgo.io.
Tistory Korean blogging platform (Kakao); custom blog domains CNAME through blog.tistory.com to *.kgslb.com endpoints.
Tumblr blog platform custom-domain endpoint; CNAMEs terminate at domains.tumblr.com.
Uberflip content experience platform (acquired by ZoomInfo); custom domains CNAME to secure.pageserve.co.
Weglot website translation platform; translated subdomain CNAMEs resolve through *.translate-cf.weglot.io.
WordPress.com hosted blogs with custom domain mapping; CNAMEs terminate at lb.wordpress.com.
Firebase Hosting legacy custom-domain target; subdomains CNAME to ``*.firebaseapp.com`` before the platform serves the SPA / static site.
Firebase Hosting modern custom-domain target; subdomains CNAME to ``*.web.app`` for Firebase-hosted apps provisioned after 2018.
Google Cloud Functions HTTPS trigger endpoints; customer-mapped subdomains CNAME to ``<function>-<hash>-<region>.cloudfunctions.net``.
Firebase Realtime Database endpoint (the JSON database, distinct from Firestore); URLs look like ``<project>.firebaseio.com``.
Looker Studio (formerly Google Data Studio) embed and report-share endpoint; custom-domain embeds proxy through this host.
Looker BI platform endpoint; customer instances are typically ``<customer>.cloud.looker.com``.
GCS custom-domain CNAME target for buckets serving static content under a CNAME-mapped subdomain; documented as the canonical target for the legacy ``c.storage.googleapis.com`` mapping.
AWS Amplify Hosting custom-domain target; frontend / SPA / SSR sites CNAME to ``<branch>.<app-id>.amplifyapp.com``.
Amazon Cognito hosted UI / user-pool domains; customer-managed domains land on ``auth.<region>.amazoncognito.com``.
AWS WAF customer-managed challenge / verification endpoints; CNAME-mapped JavaScript challenge tokens land here.
Azure Blob Storage endpoint; custom-domain-mapped containers CNAME to ``<account>.blob.core.windows.net``.
Azure Storage static-website endpoint; the ``$web`` container is served from ``<account>.z<region>.web.core.windows.net``.
Azure Static Web Apps custom-domain target; SWA-hosted sites CNAME to ``<random>.<region>.azurestaticapps.net``.
Azure Container Apps customer-facing endpoint; container apps with custom domain CNAME to ``<app>.<env>.<region>.azurecontainerapps.io``.
Azure API Management gateway endpoint; customer-managed API surfaces CNAME to ``<service>.azure-api.net``.
Azure App Service customer-facing endpoint; web apps with custom domains CNAME to ``<app>.azurewebsites.net`` before App Service serves the response.
OCI customer-facing endpoint (generic); compute / load-balancer / object-storage subdomains CNAME to ``*.oraclecloud.com`` regional / service hosts.
OCI Web Application Firewall / edge redirect endpoint; protected or redirected custom hostnames can CNAME into ``redirect.waf.oci.oraclecloud.net`` or related OCI WAF service hosts.
OCI Web Application Firewall legacy WAAS endpoint; customer-facing protected hostnames can CNAME into ``*.o.waas.oci.oraclecloud.net`` service hosts.
Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications (ERP / HCM / SCM) tenant endpoint; instances CNAME to ``<tenant>.fa.<region>.oraclecloud.com``.
IBM Cloud customer-facing endpoint covering Code Engine, Cloud Foundry, and Cloud Functions; apps CNAME to ``<app>.<region>.cf.appdomain.cloud`` or similar.
IBM Cloud legacy (Bluemix-era) endpoint; older apps still resolve through ``*.mybluemix.net``.
Alibaba Cloud API Gateway endpoint; managed APIs CNAME to ``<app>.<region>.alicloudapi.com``.
Alibaba Cloud CDN edge endpoint; accelerated subdomains CNAME to ``*.alikunlun.com``.
Alibaba Cloud CDN global server load balancing endpoint; high-volume edges CNAME through ``*.cdngslb.com``.
Alibaba Cloud generic customer-facing endpoint (compute, OSS, RDS); subdomains CNAME to ``*.<region>.aliyuncs.com``.
Railway deployment endpoint; service URLs CNAME to ``<service>.up.railway.app``.

Replit hosted-deployment endpoint CNAME (deployments CNAME to `<name>.replit.app`). Indicates the org runs at least one Replit Deployment on a branded surface. Does not by itself indicate the language or runtime; Replit hosts arbitrary user workloads.

Replit legacy hosted-Repl endpoint CNAME (older Repls resolve through `repl.co`). Indicates the org runs a Repl on the pre-deployments surface. Functionally equivalent to the newer `replit.app` chain for the binding signal.

Glitch hosted-app endpoint CNAME (projects resolve at `<project>.glitch.me`). Indicates the org runs at least one Glitch project. Does not by itself indicate production use; Glitch is most commonly used for prototypes and demos.
Zscaler ZIA / ZPA edge endpoint; tenant connectors CNAME to ``<tenant>.<cloud>.zscaler.net`` (and the numbered variants below).
Zscaler ZIA edge endpoint on the "three" cloud; tenant routing alternates between numbered clouds.

Zscaler ZIA edge-endpoint CNAME on the legacy `two` cloud. Indicates the org routes traffic through Zscaler's secure-web-gateway service. Functionally equivalent to other Zscaler cloud zones; the `two` designation reflects deployment region, not product.
Netskope SSE customer-facing endpoint; tenant gateways CNAME to ``*.gw.netskope.com``.

Netskope alternate customer-facing endpoint CNAME for the proxy-mode SWG / SSE surface (a sibling zone to `netskope.com`). Indicates the org runs Netskope for secure-web-gateway or CASB workloads. Does not by itself distinguish the active product line.
Cato Networks SASE PoP endpoint; tenant traffic CNAMEs through Cato's named PoPs (``*.cato-networks.com``).

Palo Alto Prisma Access SASE service-portal and tenant-endpoint CNAME. Indicates the org runs Prisma Access as its SASE layer. Does not distinguish Prisma Access Cloud Managed from Panorama Managed; the chain is shared.
OneLogin SSO portal and identity-provider endpoint; customer tenants land at ``<tenant>.onelogin.com``.
JumpCloud directory and SSO endpoint; portal subdomains CNAME to ``console.jumpcloud.com``.
Duo Security (Cisco) MFA / Universal Prompt endpoint; tenant frames load from ``api-<id>.duosecurity.com``.

Subdomain CNAME terminates at Socrata's hosted open-data platform (Tyler Technologies). Indicates the org publishes open-data portals on Socrata, often for government or civic transparency. Does not by itself distinguish FedRAMP from commercial deployments; the region-specific prefix (e.g. ``aws-us-east-1-fedramp-prod.socrata.net``) carries that detail.

Adobe Analytics first-party tracking endpoint (``<companyId>.sc.omtrdc.net``). Indicates the org has configured first-party data collection through Adobe Analytics. Does not distinguish Adobe Analytics from Customer Journey Analytics; both products surface through the same CNAME family.

Legacy Omniture (now Adobe Analytics) tracking domain; pre-2017 Adobe SiteCatalyst deployments use a numeric partner-ID host such as ``122.2o7.net``. Indicates a long-standing Adobe Analytics integration that has not been migrated to ``omtrdc.net``. Persistence beyond decommissioning is common; not a freshness signal.

Adobe Experience Cloud data-collection endpoint, the Experience Platform Edge Network successor to legacy tracking domains. Indicates the org runs at least one Adobe Experience Cloud product (Analytics, Target, Audience Manager, Real-Time CDP). Does not identify which product specifically; further evidence (Target mbox, AAM dpid) is needed to narrow.

SafeBase customer trust-portal endpoint (``portals.safebase.io`` or ``<org>.safebase.io``). Indicates the org publishes a SafeBase-hosted trust portal for security questionnaires, SOC reports, and compliance artefacts. Does not by itself imply SOC 2 or ISO 27001 certification; the portal can host evidence at any maturity level.

AWS regional service endpoint in us-east-1 not classified by a more specific AWS slug (ELB, S3, API Gateway, etc.). Indicates the chain terminates inside AWS's N. Virginia region but the specific service is not yet catalogued. Generic fallback; corroborate with service-specific evidence before claiming a product binding.
AWS regional service endpoint in us-west-1 not classified by a more specific AWS slug. Same caveat as us-east-1: the chain is inside AWS N. California but the specific service is uncatalogued.
AWS regional service endpoint in us-east-2 not classified by a more specific AWS slug. Same caveat as the other regional fallbacks.
AWS regional service endpoint in ap-southeast-2 (Sydney) not classified by a more specific AWS slug. Same caveat as the other regional fallbacks.

Subdomain CNAMEs into Esri's ArcGIS Hub or ArcGIS Online platform (``hub.arcgis.com`` for hub sites, ``*.arcgis.com`` for tenant-mapped pages). Indicates the org publishes geographic data or maps through ArcGIS. Does not distinguish ArcGIS Hub from ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise; the cloud-hosted family shares the chain.

Yahoo Japan image CDN endpoint (``g.yimg.jp``, ``s.yimg.jp``). Indicates the chain serves content through Yahoo Japan's media-asset infrastructure, common for Japanese commercial and partner integrations. Does not by itself indicate ownership; the CDN can be reached through partnership without the org being a direct customer.

Microsoft Edge / Azure Front Door customer-facing CDN endpoint (``<id>.dc-msedge.net``). Indicates the chain terminates in Microsoft's global edge network, the backbone for both Azure Front Door and Microsoft consumer properties. Does not distinguish Azure Front Door tenant configurations from Microsoft first-party usage; further Azure evidence is needed to claim AFD customer status.

Azion Edge customer endpoint (``<id>.ha.azioncdn.net``). Indicates the org delivers content through Azion's Brazil-based edge platform. Does not distinguish Azion products (CDN, Edge Application, Edge Firewall); the host reflects shared edge infrastructure across the product family.

Baidu's CDN and load-balancer endpoint (``<shard>.shifen.com``, with shard letters covering a/b/c/.../z and longer aliases). Indicates the chain resolves through Baidu's edge infrastructure. Common for Chinese commercial sites and partner integrations. Does not by itself identify a specific Baidu product (Baidu Cloud CDN, Smart Cloud, etc.).

JD.com (Jingdong) internal GSLB load-balancing platform. Pattern is ``<orig-fqdn>.gslb.qianxun.com`` where the original hostname is prepended to the GSLB zone. Indicates JD ecosystem presence: either a JD subsidiary, a JD partner integration, or content fronted by JD's edge platform. Does not imply the org is a Qianxun Positioning customer (the domain qianxun.com resolves to JD infrastructure, not Qianxun Positioning / 千寻位置).

Tencent EdgeOne (边缘安全加速平台 EO) customer endpoint, shard 1 of the multi-zone CNAME chain. Indicates the org runs Tencent EdgeOne for CDN, WAF, or edge-acceleration workloads. Tencent EdgeOne supersedes Tencent Cloud CDN for new tenants. Sibling shards (dnse2 through dnse5) below.
Tencent EdgeOne customer endpoint, shard 2.
Tencent EdgeOne customer endpoint, shard 3.
Tencent EdgeOne customer endpoint, shard 4.
Tencent EdgeOne customer endpoint, shard 5.

Naver Cloud Platform customer endpoint (``lb.naverncp.com`` for load balancers, with NCP service-specific subzones). Indicates the org runs workloads on Naver's South-Korea commercial cloud. Does not distinguish NCP product family (compute, CDN, AI); further evidence is needed to narrow.

Naver's internal CDN and GSLB layer; pattern is ``<orig-fqdn>.nheos.com`` where the original hostname is prepended to the Naver-owned zone. Often fronts Naver first-party properties or Naver sub-brands. Third-party tenants are rare. Does not imply the org is a Naver Cloud Platform customer; the two products are distinct (see ``naver-cloud-platform``).

Alibaba / Taobao image and asset cache CDN (``danuoyi.tbcache.com``). Indicates the chain serves Taobao-ecosystem content through Alibaba's media cache infrastructure. Common for Taobao, Tmall, and Alibaba partner sites. Does not by itself indicate the org is an Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun) customer.

Bilibili (B站) content delivery network endpoint (``w.bilicdn1.com`` and similar). Indicates the chain serves Bilibili-ecosystem media (video, image, static assets) through Bilibili's edge. Common for Bilibili partner content integrations. Does not imply the org runs a Bilibili creator channel; the CDN can deliver embedded content without an account binding.

Sina Corp's main domain appearing in a CNAME chain. Typically indicates a partner integration with Sina Weibo, Sina News, or another Sina property, but the specific binding is ambiguous from the CNAME alone. Confidence is intentionally low; corroborate before asserting a product relationship.

WeChat (微信) frontend or Mini Program domain referenced in a CNAME chain. Typically indicates a WeChat Mini Program domain-verification binding or partner integration. Does not by itself indicate WeChat Work (Qiyeweixin) or Tencent Meeting; those products use different domains.

Vanta Trust customer endpoint (``cname.vantatrust.com``, ``<org>.vantatrust.com``). Indicates the org publishes a Vanta-hosted trust portal for security questionnaires, audit reports, and compliance posture. Does not imply Vanta is the org's primary GRC platform; trust portals can be a thin layer over other compliance workflows.

GoodData embedded analytics platform endpoint (``secure.gooddata.com``, ``<tenant>.gooddata.com``). Indicates the org embeds GoodData BI dashboards in its product or runs GoodData for internal analytics. Does not distinguish GoodData Cloud from GoodData CN (on-prem); the cloud-hosted SaaS shares the suffix.

Coherent Path content-marketing AI platform endpoint (``cdn.coherentpath.com``). Indicates the org uses Coherent Path for personalized email and content recommendations. Acquired by Bounteous in 2022; the domain remains the product chain.

Blink frontline workforce app endpoint (``accelerator.blink.app``). Indicates the org runs Blink for frontline employee communication or ops workflows. Different from ``blinkdb`` or any infrastructure tool using a similar name; the ``.app`` TLD is the load- bearing signal.

Red Shield Internet web-application protection endpoint (``cdn.red-shield.net``). Indicates the org fronts at least one web property through Red Shield's WAF/CDN. Niche New Zealand based product; corroborate vendor identity before claiming an enterprise binding.

Redfin partner or affiliate frontend endpoint (``frontdoor.rdfn.net``). Typically indicates a real-estate partner integration with Redfin's listing and IDX platform. Does not imply the org is Redfin itself; the CNAME pattern is the partner-frontend chain.

Joopbox service endpoint (``srv.joopbox.com``); the specific product is not well-documented publicly. Indicates the chain terminates at Joopbox infrastructure. Confidence is intentionally low pending vendor identification; corroborate before asserting a product binding.

OVS CDN scheduler endpoint (``sched.ovscdns.com``); the specific product family is not well-documented publicly. Indicates the chain resolves through OVS's edge. Confidence is intentionally low pending vendor identification; corroborate before claiming a product family.

250ok email-deliverability analytics endpoint (``<id>.250analytics.com``). Acquired by Validity in 2020 and folded into Validity Everest; the legacy domain remains the chain. Indicates the org runs deliverability monitoring, often alongside DMARC reporting and inbox placement testing. Does not imply Validity Everest is the org's primary email vendor.

Subdomain CNAMEd to a Discourse-hosted community forum (`*.hosted-by-discourse.com`). Indicates the org runs a hosted Discourse community on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only; it does not indicate forum activity or member count.

Custom domain CNAMEd to a Substack publication (`*.substack-custom-domains.com`). Indicates the org publishes a newsletter on Substack under a branded domain. Does not indicate subscriber count or paid-tier status.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Document360 knowledge base (`*.document360.io`). Indicates a hosted help/KB portal on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a StatusPal hosted status page (`*.statuspal.io`). Indicates the org publishes a service-status page on a branded domain. Does not indicate incident history.
CNAME into a Cloudsmith hosted package/artifact registry (`*.cloudsmith.io`). Indicates the org distributes software artifacts through Cloudsmith. Infrastructure-binding signal only.
Custom domain CNAMEd to a Tally form (`cname.tally.so`). Indicates the org hosts forms/surveys on Tally under a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Branded short-link / social CNAME into Oktopost (`okt.to`), a B2B social-media management platform. Indicates the org runs social campaigns through Oktopost. Marketing-tooling signal only.

Subdomain CNAMEd to a BeyondTrust cloud instance (`*.beyondtrustcloud.com`), a privileged-access / remote-support platform. Indicates the org runs BeyondTrust for PAM or secure remote support. Does not distinguish the specific product.
Subdomain CNAMEd to BeyondTrust Remote Support on the legacy Bomgar cloud (`*.bomgarcloud.com`). Same vendor as beyondtrustcloud.com; indicates secure remote support. Hosting-binding signal only.

Subdomain CNAMEd to Arctic Wolf infrastructure (`*.arcticwolf.net`), a managed detection and response (MDR) provider. Suggests the org uses Arctic Wolf for security operations. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to Rootly (`*.rootly.com`), an incident-management platform. Indicates the org runs incident response tooling on Rootly. Hosting-binding signal only.

Subdomain CNAMEd to Material Security (`*.material.security`), an email security and data-governance platform layered over M365 / Google Workspace. Suggests the org runs Material for mailbox protection. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom domain CNAMEd to a Bynder digital-asset-management portal (`*.getbynder.com`). Indicates the org runs a Bynder DAM on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom domain CNAMEd to a Bynder DAM portal on the primary `bynder.com` domain (alongside the getbynder.com variant). Same vendor; hosting-binding signal only.
Custom domain CNAMEd to a Brandfolder digital-asset-management portal (`*.mybrandfolder.com`). Indicates the org runs a Brandfolder DAM on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Tracking / landing-page CNAME into Act-On (`*.actonsoftware.com`), a marketing-automation platform. Indicates the org runs Act-On campaigns. Marketing-tooling signal only.

Login subdomain CNAMEd to Janrain / Akamai Identity Cloud (`*.janrain.ws`), a customer identity and access management (CIAM) platform. Indicates the org runs hosted CIAM. Does not distinguish the specific Akamai product tier.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Cision MediaRoom online newsroom (`*.mediaroom.com`). Indicates the org runs a hosted press / investor-relations newsroom. Hosting-binding signal only.
Tracking CNAME into Impact.com (`*.impactradius.com`), a partnership / affiliate-marketing platform. Indicates the org runs partnership programs through Impact. Marketing-tooling signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to PartnerPage (`*.partnerpage.io`), a hosted partner-directory platform. Indicates the org runs a branded partner directory. Hosting-binding signal only.
Subdomain CNAMEd to Mynewsdesk (`*.mynewsdesk.com`), a PR / newsroom and press-distribution platform. Indicates the org runs a hosted newsroom. Hosting-binding signal only.

Autodiscover / service CNAME terminating in the Microsoft 365 US Government cloud (`*.usgovcloud.microsoft`), i.e. GCC High / DoD rather than the commercial cloud. Indicates a government-cloud M365 tenant. Distinct from the commercial microsoft365 slug.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Freshservice (Freshworks) IT service management portal (`*.freshservice.com`). Indicates the org runs Freshservice ITSM on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to Avature (`*.avature.net`), a talent / recruiting CRM platform. Indicates the org runs Avature for hiring or candidate-relationship management. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to a Help Scout Docs knowledge base (`*.helpscoutdocs.com`). Indicates the org runs a Help Scout hosted help center on a branded domain. Hosting-binding signal only.
Custom subdomain CNAMEd to Oracle Service Cloud / RightNow (`*.custhelp.com`), a customer-service / help-center platform. Indicates the org runs Oracle Service Cloud. Hosting-binding signal only.
API endpoint CNAMEd to TIBCO Mashery (`*.mashery.com`), an API-management / gateway platform. Indicates the org fronts APIs through Mashery. Hosting-binding signal only.
Subdomain CNAMEd to Crownpeak (`*.crownpeak.net`), a digital-experience / CMS and consent-management platform. Indicates the org runs Crownpeak. Hosting-binding signal only.
Tracking / messaging CNAME into Infobip (`*.infobip.com`), a cloud-communications (CPaaS) provider. Indicates the org sends messaging / email through Infobip. Hosting-binding signal only.

Email tracking or messaging CNAME into Infobip (`*.email-messaging.com`), including regional tracking hosts. Indicates the org routes email tracking or CPaaS messaging surface through Infobip. Hosting-binding signal only.
Ad-serving CNAME into Ad Legend (`*.adlegend.com`), a digital advertising / ad-management platform. Indicates the org serves ads through Ad Legend. Marketing-tooling signal only.
Piano (subscription / paywall) CNAME target (`tinypass.com`). Common on news / media apexes for paywall authentication.
Validity Everest (deliverability engagement tracking) CNAME target (`everestengagement.com`).
Edgecast (Verizon Media, now Edgio) CDN CNAME target (`edgecastcdn.net`). Indicates the subdomain is fronted by Edgecast's edge network.

Edgecast (Verizon Digital Media, now Edgio) CDN CNAME target (`zetacdn.net`, e.g. `wpc.<account>.zetacdn.net`). The same edge network as `edgecastcdn.net` under a second infrastructure domain; indicates the subdomain is fronted by Edgecast/Edgio.
Adestra MessageFocus (Upland Software) CNAME target (`msgfocus.com`). Indicates the subdomain is bound to an Adestra tenant for marketing email.
Gorgias (helpdesk for e-commerce) CNAME target (`gorgias.help`). Indicates the subdomain hosts a Gorgias helpdesk.
Shopee (e-commerce, SEA) CNAME target (`shopeemobile.com`). Indicates the subdomain CNAMEs to Shopee infrastructure.
Cleverbridge (e-commerce / subscription billing) CNAME target (`cleverbridge.com`). Indicates the subdomain hosts a Cleverbridge checkout or account portal.
Blackbaud Convio (nonprofit fundraising) CNAME target (`convio.net`).
Ovative Group (marketing analytics) CNAME target (`ovative.com`).
ThreatMetrix (LexisNexis fraud detection) CNAME target (`online-metrix.net`). Indicates the subdomain proxies device- fingerprinting traffic.
BigCommerce (e-commerce platform) CNAME target (`mybigcommerce.com`). Indicates the subdomain hosts a BigCommerce store.
Rio SEO (local-search marketing) CNAME target (`rioseo.com`).

SAP Cloud Platform (Kyma / Shoot) CNAME target (`k8s-hana.ondemand.com`). Indicates the subdomain is hosted on SAP's managed Kubernetes offering. Narrowed from `ondemand.com` in v1.9.24 QA to avoid shadowing sap-commerce.
Archbee (developer documentation) CNAME target (`archbee.com`). Indicates the subdomain hosts public docs.
Storm Reply (Reply Group SAP consulting / cloud platform) CNAME target (`stormreply.com`).
Cloudways (managed cloud hosting) CNAME target (`cloudwaysapps.com`). SaaS hosting on top of AWS / DigitalOcean / Linode / Vultr.
BrightSites (B2B e-commerce sites) CNAME target (`mybrightsites.com`).
Cirrus Identity (higher-education federation bridge) CNAME target (`cirrusidentity.com`). Common on .edu apexes for Shibboleth / Duo / Google bridge.

Subdomain CNAME terminating in the Australian Access Federation's `aaf.edu.au` namespace. AAF documents customer hostname CNAMEs for its hosted Rapid IdP service. This indicates an AAF-operated public identity surface, not the namespace owner's complete identity stack.
Pressable (managed WordPress hosting) CNAME target (`pressdns.com`). SaaS hosting for WordPress.
Sanity SVD (image / asset CDN) CNAME target (`svdcdn.com`). Sanity's structured-content delivery edge.
INAP (Internap, now part of EvocativeIT) bare-metal / colo CNAME target (`internap.com`).
BusinessWire (press release distribution) CNAME target (`businesswire.com`). Common on investor-relations subdomains.
Gannett Digital (USA Today Network) CNAME target (`gannettdigital.com`).
Sourcepoint (consent management platform / CMP) CNAME target (`sp-prod.net`).
Dub (link management) CNAME target (`dub.co`). Indicates the subdomain is a Dub-managed shortlink endpoint.
Hund (status page platform) CNAME target (`hund.io`).

Does not prove the domain processes payments through Stripe. The verification is administrative and persists independently of transaction activity.

DocuSign domain-verification TXT (the bare `docusign=` prefix). Indicates the domain is administratively bound to a DocuSign account, typically for SSO federation, account claim, or DocuSign Click custom-domain setups. Does not prove active envelope-sending volume.

Google site-verification TXT for Google Search Console or Google site-ownership claim. Indicates someone has proven ownership of the domain to Google for a search or webmaster surface. Does not indicate Google Workspace email or identity adoption; the same TXT prefix is used across many Google products and is the lowest-signal Google binding.

CNAME terminating at `instructure.com`, the canonical Canvas LMS hosted-platform domain. Indicates the org's learning subdomain (commonly `learn.<edu>` or `canvas.<edu>`) is hosted by Instructure. Does not by itself indicate the integration depth (SCIM, LTI, federated SSO); those are configured separately inside the Canvas tenant.

Canvas LMS domain-verification TXT. Indicates the org has claimed the domain inside an Instructure tenant, typically as the SSO and custom-domain precondition. Does not prove active enrollment; verification can persist on a domain after the tenant is wound down.

CNAME terminating at `blackboard.com`, indicating Blackboard Learn LMS hosts a learning subdomain for the org. Does not distinguish self-hosted from Blackboard SaaS; the CNAME alone is consistent with both. Cross-check with the Blackboard Collaborate (`bbcollab.com`) chain for live-classroom adoption.

CNAME terminating at `bbcollab.com`, Blackboard Collaborate live-classroom hosted-platform domain. Indicates the org has live-session capabilities on top of Blackboard Learn. Does not indicate volume; a single classroom tenant produces the same CNAME as district-wide rollout.

CNAME into `moodle.com`, indicating Moodle LMS hosting (either self-hosted with `moodle.com`-tracked services or a Moodle partner-hosted tenant). Does not prove which Moodle version is running; the CNAME is the platform-binding signal, not a version probe.

CNAME into `moodlecloud.com`, Moodle's managed SaaS offering. Indicates the org is on Moodle's managed cloud rather than self-hosted Moodle. Distinct from the `moodle.com` chain; typically present on smaller-deployment K-12 or extension programs.

CNAME terminating at `ellucian.com`, the canonical Ellucian Banner Student Information System hosted-platform domain. Indicates the org uses Banner SIS for enrollment, records, or financial aid. Does not indicate the integration topology; Banner is often middlewared behind an integration hub like Ethos or a campus ESB.

CNAME terminating at `ellucianworkflow.com`, Ellucian's Workflow process-automation product. Indicates the org has stood up business-process workflows on top of Banner. Does not prove the workflows are active; the CNAME persists after tenant claim independent of usage.

CNAME terminating at `joinhandshake.com`, the Handshake student-career-services hosted-platform domain. Indicates the org publishes career-services content via Handshake. Does not prove employer-side activity; the chain is a school-side tenant signal.

CNAME into `tophat.com`, the Top Hat student-engagement / course-engagement hosted-platform domain. Indicates the org uses Top Hat for in-class polling, attendance, or homework. Does not indicate course volume; a single department's adoption produces the same CNAME as institution-wide rollout.

Salesforce Nonprofit Success Pack domain-verification TXT. Indicates the org runs NPSP on top of a Salesforce org for donor and program management. Does not prove active fundraising volume; NPSP is a managed-package layer on Salesforce, and the verification ships at install time.

CNAME into `salesforce-communities.com`, the Salesforce Experience Cloud (formerly Communities) hosted-platform domain. Commonly used by nonprofits for donor portals and volunteer signup. Does not prove the community is public-facing; many sit behind invitation-only auth.

CNAME terminating at `blackbaud.com`, indicating Blackbaud's CRM, Raiser's Edge, or fundraising-tool surface is hosted for the org. Does not distinguish the specific Blackbaud product; Raiser's Edge, eTapestry, and NetCommunity all share this chain root.

CNAME into `blackbaudhosting.com`, Blackbaud's managed-services hosting domain. Indicates the org runs Blackbaud as a hosted managed service rather than self-hosted. Does not by itself indicate product mix; cross-check with the `blackbaud.com` chain.

CNAME terminating at `classy.org`, Classy's nonprofit fundraising platform, now GoFundMe Pro. Indicates the org's donation pages are hosted on Classy. Does not indicate fundraising volume; a single campaign produces the same chain as a continuous program.

Branded fundraising subdomain pointing to the documented `vanity.classy.org` domain-masking target for GoFundMe Pro, previously Classy. It indicates hosted campaign pages, not campaign activity or fundraising volume.
